This is one of the places to go for art resources. We would usually go to the Art-Fren and popular here. But since that was the usual places, we decided to venture out abit this time..

There are different lifts for commercial and residential. If you are heading up to the shops, you can also take the escalator up. Its rather easily accessible to many vendors, so you can shop around from one to another.

One more thing to note, carpark is slightly lower than normal. There are visual signs of vehicles hitting the beam within the carpark itself. So if you do have a vehicle higher than 1.8m, we would suggest to avoid coming and parking here.

Check out our images for the ways around this complex. And hope you guys don't get lost..

Cheers!

Bras Basah Complex feels like a surviving time capsule from Singapore in the 1980s and 1990s. Walking through the corridors brought back memories of old-school bookshops, watch repair stores, art supply shops, music stores, printing services, and hobby retailers that used to be much more common in Singapore. It is honestly quite surprising - and refreshing - that this place is still around despite all the redevelopment happening elsewhere

The building itself has a very nostalgic charm with its open-air design, older architecture, and quieter atmosphere compared to modern malls. It may not be flashy, but that is part of the appeal. There is something enjoyable about slowly wandering around and discovering long-established independent shops that have clearly been operating for decades

Seen better days than now. Used to be a popular place for art supplies and books. Still has a nostalgic feel about the place as it brings back memories where once students & parents flood the place shopping for books & stationary supplies. Same for musicians & art frantic scouring for instruments & inspiration.
Within lies a food court and some restaurants. Basement has limited parking which cost an out $1.20 per 30mins.

Food court was POPPING when I came!

I got the golden soup beef (Monday special) but they have specials everyday. Soup was great but it could have more meat.

The last two stores had long lines (with office workers), so they must be good. I will come back to try them the next time I'm back in Singapore!

Bugis Street is one of the best places to visit in Singapore for shopping, street food, and local vibes. The area is always lively and full of energy, with many affordable fashion items, souvenirs, snacks, and accessories to explore. Really suitable for tourists or anyone who enjoys walking around and discovering unique shops.

There are also many food stalls and nearby cafés around Bugis, making it a great place to relax after shopping. The atmosphere becomes even more interesting at night with the bright lights and busy crowd. Convenient location, easy to access by MRT, and a nice place to experience the modern and local side of Singapore at the same time. Highly recommended for shopping lovers and first-time visitors to Singapore.

I absolutely love this traditional market. It sells all kinds of goods, modern and traditional items, food and drinks, souvenirs, and various trinkets.

There's also a large fruit stall outside. If you're staying at a nearby hotel and craving fruit, this is definitely an excellent choice, incredible value and extremely fresh.

The food options here are overwhelming. The best way is to eat while you walk, and you can definitely eat until you're full. After you're full, there are still all kinds of desserts and drinks to enjoy.

This place can easily rival traditional markets and night markets from around the world. It's highly recommended for a visit!

The street is always bustling with energy, filled with countless small shops and stalls offering trendy clothes, accessories, souvenirs, and much more at affordable prices. It’s a great place for budget shopping and picking up unique items.
Even if you’re not planning to shop a lot, just walking through the market is an experience in itself. There are also plenty of food stalls and drink options around, so you can enjoy local snacks and refreshing juices while exploring.
It can get quite crowded, especially in the evenings, so be prepared for that. But overall, it’s a must-visit place in Singapore for shopping lovers and anyone who enjoys vibrant street markets!

We went to check on the souvenir prices and among all souvenir places we’ve been, we think bugis street has the best prices already. Super affordable and not so much crowd. You can haggle a little and have some food and snacks that you can try around while shopping.

Bugis Street is a lively and bustling shopping spot that a lot of visitors recommend for a fun and budget-friendly experience. Many people love how wide and varied the shops are, with everything from affordable fashion and accessories to quirky souvenirs, making it a great place to explore and shop without spending too much.
The street food stalls and snack options add to the vibrant atmosphere — you can easily spend some time tasting local bites while browsing through the stalls. It does get crowded, especially in the evenings and on weekends, but that’s part of the energetic vibe here.
Overall, it’s a great place for casual shopping, good street food, and vibrant street life — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Bugis Street ek bahut lively aur bustling shopping destination hai jise kaafi log budget-friendly shopping ke liye recommend karte hain. Yahan shops ka variety wide hai — saste fashion, accessories, souvenirs aur unique items milte hain, jis se shopping explore karna fun ho jata hai.
Street food stalls aur local snacks bhi yahan ka vibe aur enhance karte hain — shopping ke beech mein local bites try karna easy aur enjoyable hota hai. Thoda crowd hota hai, especially evenings aur weekends par, lekin yeh Bugis Street ka energetic charm hi hai.
Overall, casual shopping, good street food aur vibrant atmosphere ke liye ek badiya place — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Range of products at store is wide. But what stood out my shopping experience is cashier Josephine’s going to the extra mile in two cases. When I asked for the location of plant- based meat at the supermarket and when I want to pay and she told me of a cash voucher for Yuu members. Really really impressed with her earnestly wanting to help me and in  made  my shopping experience a memorably and happy one ! Keep it up Ms Josephine!
A good place for locals and tourists (even for vegans and vegetarians) to get almost anything,. Merlion stuff is also available at this supermarket. Happy Shopping.

Situated in the heart of Bugis, Cold Storage in Bugis Junction Basement stands as a spacious supermarket, offering a wide array of items that cater to almost every daily need. The selection is vast, ensuring you can find everything you require conveniently under one roof.

The freshness of fruits and vegetables is a notable highlight, promising quality produce for shoppers. Keep an eye out for promotions on specific items, making your shopping experience not only comprehensive but cost-effective.

For those seeking a quick and efficient checkout process, self-checkout machines are readily available, streamlining your shopping journey. Cold Storage in Bugis Junction Basement is not just a supermarket; it's a go-to destination for a seamless and satisfying shopping experience.

I bought two pieces of Arla salted butter yesterday, only to find both completely mouldy. While they were on promotion and nearing expiry (today, in fact, which we didn’t know), this is absolutely unacceptable, irresponsible and unhygienic. It’s frankly disgusting that these were still on the shelves. I expect better quality control and basic food safety standards from your store.

Nothing much there actually other then your normal groceries and daily needs. I ordered the salad at the diy salad stand ingredients were alright wish they had stock up more sauces as I wanted Cesar and they don’t have it so I had to go with another dressing though the staff was very friendly they forgot to add some of the ingredients requested in the paper slip. Other then that all is alright

place_name: Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ 思家客武吉士店
review_text:
ʜᴇʟʟᴏ ʜᴇʟʟᴏ, ᴍᴇᴇᴛ ᴀɢᴀɪɴ ᴡɪᴛʜ ᴍᴇ, ᴀɴᴛʜᴏɴy. ɪ'ᴍ ɢᴏᴏɢʟᴇ ʀᴇᴠɪᴇᴡᴇʀ ꜰʀᴏᴍ ɪɴᴅᴏɴᴇꜱɪᴀ.

Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ (思家客武吉士店) Review

Located on the second floor of Bugis+, Scarlett Supermarket offers a wide range of Chinese snacks, beverages, and instant meals. It’s a go-to spot for anyone looking to stock up on unique Chinese products you might not easily find elsewhere in Singapore.

What’s Good:

Huge variety: From popular spicy hotpot bases and instant noodles to trending snacks like Lay’s localized flavors and White Rabbit candies, the selection is impressive.

Affordable pricing: Prices are generally reasonable, and there are often promotions or bundle deals.

Convenient location: Right in the heart of Bugis, it’s super accessible.

Clean and well-organized: The store layout makes browsing enjoyable even when it’s a bit crowded.

What Could Be Better:

Crowded during peak hours: Especially on weekends, it can feel a bit cramped.

Limited fresh items: It’s mostly packaged goods—don’t expect fresh produce or cooked meals here.

Some items lack English labels: Might be tough for non-Mandarin speakers to figure out what certain things are.

Verdict:
A fun and vibrant place to explore Chinese snacks and pantry staples. Great for both nostalgic shoppers and curious snackers. Worth a visit if you're in Bugis and want something a bit different from your usual supermarket run.

God Bless

place_name: Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ 思家客武吉士店
review_text:
Just opened recently . You can get your snacks from China here plus groceries.
You can get freshly make burgers , Shen Jian Bao .
For $5 a box you can get vege options of pickled vegetables and $10 for meat variety such as sliced pork and beef tripe .

place_name: Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ 思家客武吉士店
review_text: bought a whole bunch of hot foods, the friendly guy manning the hot foods station was SO friendly & kind, he even gave us free sweet potato balls to try w/o expecting that we will buy, he was so approachable and chatty too! and the foods are delicious and REALLY affordable, the 小酥肉 super fresh and juicy, SUPER generous portion, sweet potato balls SUPER good not the typical airy kind, and the 生煎包 is pretty good as well, yums!!

place_name: Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ 思家客武吉士店
review_text:
Love scarlett for cheap drinks and snacks, and this one has a hot food section inside.

It's hard to find places with tea eggs nowadays! But the tea egg here disappointingly just taste like normal boiled egg :C other reviewers seem to enjoy their other hot foods so might give them a try if im in the area again, but the tea eggs r disappointing.

Just go opposite to bugis street, and find the shop with a little tea egg pot instead, $1 for one egg there and more legit

place_name: Scarlett Supermarket @ Bugis+ 思家客武吉士店
review_text:
Good deals at the supermarket. Can't say the same about the fast food inside though.. I think coming here for the supermarket only would be better.

Eating area is quite bad, not cleaned. Still, the food is decent for decent price but I would rather pay for a McSpicy meal if eat-in. Maybe it would be OK if you're just ordering takeaway.
Near my hotel, have food and drink. Even they sell covid test and medicine. Have breakfast here can save alot money during travel.
Except for the seats without dining, everything else is good.
Good location and I got the disposable raincoat for universal studio:) Very happy!
Good location. I got what i crave for.

Absolutely tiny 7-Eleven but had at least some selections of most of the basics we wanted and opens 24/7 as you would expect. Those looking for a bigger 7-Eleven would do well to head to the other end of Bencoolen and there is a much bigger store by the Ibis hotel.
Staff was very rude when asking him a question

What a market! Clothing, food, drinks, souvenirs, toys, electricals, shoes & much much more. Stalls are right next to each other & there must have been over 500! We also bought some fresh jackfruit from a stall at the rear of the market & they cut the fruit & packaged it whilst you were ordering . Fantastic.

Bugis Market @ Bugis Street is a vibrant and bustling spot packed with a huge selection of shops.
It’s very popular with both tourists and locals.
You’ll find fruit stalls, souvenirs, street food, mobile phone services, chocolates, sweets, T-shirts, trinkets, beauty services, bags, pharmacy and plenty more.
It’s always busy and crowded, but over the years, the place feels cleaner and less stuffy, with better air circulation now.
Worth a visit to browse around and soak in the lively atmosphere.

A vibrant and lively shopping destination in the heart of Singapore. Bugis Market offers a wide variety of affordable clothing, accessories, souvenirs, snacks, and local products. It is a great place to explore, shop, and experience the bustling atmosphere of Singapore.
The market is well connected by public transport, and there are plenty of food options nearby. A must-visit destination for both tourists and locals looking for good bargains and a unique shopping experience.
Highly recommended!

Bugis Street is a fantastic destination for tourists looking to pick up Singapore mementos, ornaments, apparel, and gifts. The prices are much more reasonable compared to other parts of Singapore, making it a great place for budget shopping. Keep in mind that prices can vary from shop to shop, so it's definitely worth browsing through a few stalls before making your final purchase!

The fruit selection was vast and fresh, and the prices were cheaper than in many other places. Luckily, it was durian season today, and I bought three boxes of Musang King durians for only 30 yuan – so satisfying!

Such a lovely women-only gym space. You can really feel the care that has gone into setting everything up. The gym is clean, tidy, and feels very comfortable to be in, which makes such a difference when working out.

The equipment is well kept and thoughtfully arranged, with everything you need without the space feeling overwhelming.

I also really appreciate the welcoming and safe environment, it feels like a place where women can come and focus on themselves confidently. Wishing this new space all the best, it’s a wonderful addition and I’m sure many will love it!

Love the all girls gym space! Feels like a safe and closed space to workout and not feel shy. Location is really accessible by multiple busses and MRT.

Love that there’s even a prayer mat to pray as well! Thank you for providing a space for the girlys to work out 🤍

Love this space!! I went for their introductory class for a guided full body workout session with all the machines (sans the treadmill) and it was comprehensive and friendly for beginners. I left feeling amazing as someone who has not worked out in a gym for years.

It's a clean and roomy space for a few people to work out at the same time. And you feel safe since it's a women-only pod. The small amenities like the water dispenser and prayer mat are so thoughtful. Looking forward to working out here again!!

I’ve visited this gym pod several times now and I keep coming back for a reason, it’s just that good!
As a female, I feel so comfortable and safe working out here. The place is incredibly clean every single time I visit, you can tell they really take hygiene seriously.

All the equipment in perfect condition, which makes every workout session so much more enjoyable. What’s even better is that they offer intro class for beginners, perfect if you’re just starting out and want to learn how to use the equipment properly and pick up some basic moves. No need to feel lost or intimidated!

The water dispenser is super convenient, and I really appreciate that they provide a praying mat, such a thoughtful touch that makes this space truly inclusive 🙏🏼

The location near Bugis is a huge plus, easy to get to whether you’re commuting or just nearby. Highly recommend this gym pod to all the ladies out there looking for a clean, well-equipped, and welcoming space to train! 💪🏽

Got to use the gym while it was completely empty! I really like how spacious and clean it is, plus there’s a great mix of equipment (they even had heel lifts!). Enjoyed trying out the hip thrust machine especially. There’s even a water dispenser and a cable to charge your phone which is so so thoughtful and helpful. Honestly, it’s more affordable and more well-equipped so would defo recommend this over gym pod 🤭

One of my favourite AF in town. Split over 2 floors, this is the slightly troublesome part, so bring your keyfob with you at all times. 3rd floor is most of the machines and some half-racks and smiths. 4th floor has 6 full racks, powerlifting platform, dumbbell area with db up to 50kg and 5 benches, and full cable jungle setup. Unique machines like belt squat and combined high row/ chest press combined machine. There is a functional area on 5th floor with another cable stacks.

Overall well maintained and clean. They use a nice essential oil air freshener as well. Showers on the lower floor. I like to drop by for my workout when in town as they have almost everything you need 👍🏻

Well equipped gym with good crowd. Easy access in good location. Wash rooms x 2 for each gender, one on each level.  One point though, sign is for gym users (your customers) and the language is crude and unsuitable.

Simply a more restorative way - thank you for returning the weights for other users - would be more professional.
Highly recommend! The equipment is top-notch, and the entire three story facility was impeccably clean. The customer service is also fantastic, Ahmad was welcoming and super helpful.

Anytime Fitness Bugis is easily one of the best gyms in the area. The space is clean, well-maintained, and equipped with everything you need — from free weights and functional equipment to a solid range of machines and cardio options. The staff are friendly, helpful, and always make the environment welcoming, even during busy hours.

I especially love how spacious the gym feels despite its central location. It’s easy to find a spot to work out, and the overall vibe is motivating but not overwhelming. The members here are respectful, and the atmosphere feels safe and comfortable for all fitness levels.

The 24/7 access is a huge plus, and the location is super convenient, just a short walk from Bugis MRT. If you’re looking for a clean, accessible, and well-equipped gym in the heart of Bugis, this is definitely the place to go.

AF Bugis is a pretty solid gym, lots of racks, platforms, and machines, and the space never feels too crowded. Super easy place to get a good workout in.

Big shoutout to ahmad! He’s always super friendly and helpful, and really makes the gym feel welcoming. Overall, great vibe and great staff.

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

Awesome running track with canopy! You can go easy or treshold, anything you like to go that day! No worry of raining. The colorful track also boost the feeling. Although a lil bit “effort” to go here, but it is worth it!

We attend the Monster Jam 2025, Epic trucks, but Pit Party value gap — no freebies for kids.

We paid extra for Pit Party access and walked into… a merch zone. No free keepsakes at all—just items for sale and a small colouring table. For a premium add-on aimed at families, that’s a miss.

What the Pit Party should include (at minimum):
•	Free autograph cards or mini posters for each featured truck
•	Sticker sheet or Pit Party “passport” kids can stamp at each truck
•	Lanyard/badge (souvenir access pass)
•	Foam earplugs for children (practical + appreciated)

If nothing physical is possible, at least offer a QR code for printable posters/cards and a digital pit scavenger hunt with a small prize pickup.

There’s also a two-hour dead zone from pit close (4:00 pm) to show start (6:00 pm). Schedule some pre-show content—driver Q&A, trivia on the big screen, or early-seat entertainment—so families aren’t just waiting.

On-track: the stunts were fantastic.

Audio: the PA was muddy and unintelligible for interviews/commentary—in the pit, at our seats, and around the concourse. Needs proper EQ and coverage.

Safety: our exit staircase had two failed lights. One staff member with a torch stood by the second; the first was unlit and unattended. Please fix lighting and staff both points.
Great place! Visited OCBC Arena for Singapore Smash 2026. The staff was reliable and helpful, especially Shafiq from the gym who helped me understand the gym and opening hours! 谢谢
Best place to be at in Singapore for sporting events. State of the art facilities and good crowd control throughout.

Good functional sports facilities hub and stadium. Transportation is terrific with the MRT station next to the stadium and also bus stops nearby. The stadium was well-constructed for large scale events and the effects were spectacular. Unfortunately it was raining when I was attending a concert. Instead of using the sheltered walkway, due to poor planning and organization of the organizer, the spectators were made to queue in the rain for bag check. This resulted in unnecessary hassle and unpleasantness of the experience 😒

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

Bukit Batok Swimming Complex is located within the HDB neighbourhood.

I went to swim there during the weekend. Do note that there are some lanes being used for training.

The swimming pool's facilities are well maintained and clean so far. There's a decent pool area for the kids.

The shower head is very powerful. Similar to other public swimming pools, there's no heater. There's toilet specially for kids. For the female toilet, there's this adjustable hair dryer, which is pretty cool. I don't need to hold it while it dries my hair. You need to put your hand over the sensor for it to sense, and it will start blowing. However, it might stop after a while, so you need to wave your hand again for it to work.

There are lockers if you want to lock your belongings. If not, you can put it along the seats of the pool.

There's also water cooler with icy water for you to refill your bottle or drink directly.

Overall, it's a good experience swimming in Bukit Batok Swimming Complex.

Complex has been around for many decades, almost iconic. There is a fun pool for children too! HDB parkkng available nearby.
Unfortunately, there is only 1 entrance to the complex. So always have to walk one big round if parked at nearby HDB.

- S$1.3 for adult on weekdays.

Such a little jewel of a cinema in Singapore. It has a wonderfully quaint, retro charm that makes the whole experience feel special and nostalgic in the best way. In a time when so many cinemas feel generic, this place has real character and soul. The atmosphere is intimate, cosy, and full of old-school charm, which makes watching a film here feel far more memorable. Definitely the kind of hidden gem that deserves more love and support. A must-visit for anyone who appreciates unique independent cinemas. We really gotta support places like this!
Lovely cinema! Really love the retro aesthetic of the lobby. Glad that Filmhouse is carrying on The Projector's legacy of providing a space for alternative cinema!

I am glad that there's a place like Filmhouse in Singapore to appreciate and support quality movies for the movie-fans. The selection of movies that Filmhouse is choosing to screen seem to be artistic and award-winning ones - both from the past and newly released. Follow the trail to Jelutong Tower and Tree Top Walk. Enjoy the view and the trail. Have not cover all the trails. Will be back to try and experience other trails. Good experience to spend weekends with nature. Escape from the city view.

This area consist of a network of jungle trails where you can trek. There many tracks that you can walk and the natural forest canopy provides a wonderful refreshing air with minimal pollution. The place is serene! Most of the paths are manmade now so it's easier to hike up to tree top walk! There are A LOT of monkeys do keep your loose items safe at all times, also multiple signs are put up to not feed the monkeys.

Nice place for walking and jogging. The trails are well marked. Great nature!!!

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

Serene Garden is a quiet and relaxing spot in the heart of the city. Surrounded by lush greenery and beautiful landscapes, it’s perfect for a peaceful walk or a break from the busy city life.

🌳 Nature – Enjoy the calm atmosphere and fresh air.
🧘 Relaxation – Great for meditation or just unwinding.
📸 Scenic Views – Lovely spots for photos with nature.

If you want to recharge and connect with nature, Serene Garden is the place to be!

The Serene Garden at Gardens by the Bay is a peaceful, beautifully landscaped space designed for relaxation and reflection. Featuring lush greenery, water features, and calming pathways, it provides a tranquil escape from the city. It’s a perfect spot for nature lovers seeking a quiet retreat within the vibrant garden complex.

Serene Garden @ Gardens by the Bay
The Gardens will have different decorations based on the current seasons or various festivals.
​Examples of past and present decorations:
*New Year Day, January.
*​Singapore National Day 2025.
*​United Buddy Bears exhibition at Supertree Grove Lawn in Gardens by the Bay, Singapore, until October 12, 2025.
*Mid-Autumn Festival September 2025.
*Christmas, December.

Situated at the far south of the Gardens by the Bay, Serene Garden is often missed by visitors.
🌲The sculptural rocks, white pebbles, ornamental pine trees and mounds landscape theme add a serene feel to the entire Garden.
And indeed the Garden is tranquil and peaceful.
🏞️The Garden also featured a small man-made waterfall with water cascading down into a slow flowing stream.
⛰️A stone sculpture is displayed at the south entrance Plaza.
🍃Come visit Serene Garden and enjoy it's tranquility.

This is definitely "the path less traveled" within Gardens by the Bay, but absolutely worth the visit. The garden is definitely inspired by the Japanese garden, which has lots of unique Junipers that look like Bonsai trees, bamboos, unique big rocks, benches and even Buddha head sculpture...  You definitely will feel the Zen when wandering around the garden. But to really enjoy the peacefulness, suggest to visit during non peak hours, such as early morning/late evening or weekdays.

Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

Gardens by the Bay is an amazing place to visit in Singapore. The gardens are beautifully maintained, with lots of greenery, walking paths, water features and great photo spots everywhere you look.

Even on a cloudy day after some rain, the place still looked beautiful and peaceful. The views of Marina Bay Sands and the Supertrees in the background are incredible, and there’s plenty to see just walking around the outdoor areas.

It’s clean, well organised and easy to explore. Some attractions cost extra, but the free garden areas alone are worth visiting. Definitely one of the best places to see in Singapore.

A nostalgic movie night surrounded by childhood memories. Watched Godzilla Raids Again, before the movie started, we grabbed some free coffee, tea and childhood snacks — such a sweet touch that made the whole experience even more nostalgic. Bring backs lots of of memories.

A wide collection of unique and vintage toys :) occasionally, they host movie screenings and there are event spaces allocated in the museum itself too :) Looking forward to its exciting events as a toy/anime enthusiast!!🤩🤩

This is definitely a place to call home.

The ArtScience Museum is a fascinating place that blends creativity, science, and art in a unique way. The building itself is striking, shaped like a lotus flower, and feels like a work of art even before you step inside. The exhibitions are interactive, visually stunning, and thoughtfully curated, so there’s something to engage everyone—whether you’re into technology, art, or science. I loved how immersive some of the displays were, making learning feel fun and memorable. It’s also very well maintained, with clear signage and helpful staff. Even if you’re not usually a museum person, the ArtScience Museum is a must-visit for its innovative design and inspiring exhibits.

ArtScience Museum is one of the most unique museums I’ve ever visited. The lotus-shaped building is stunning, and the Future World exhibition by teamLab is truly immersive and interactive — especially the Crystal Universe installation. It’s

The ArtScience Museum, the largest Museum of Singapore, is located in Marina Bay  in Singapore.
It was opened in 2011 by Singapore's PM - Lee Hsien Loong.

It features exhibitions that blend art, science, culture and technology.
It also hosts touring exhibitions curated by other museums, in addition to a permanent exhibition created in conjunction with art collective team Lab.

Unfortunately for us, our visit during 2023 did not reveal that in 2025, it was going for partnership with PGLang's when the museum was rebranded.

The architecture now is said to be a form reminiscent of a lotus flower, featuring an exterior made of fibre-reinforced plastic on top of a steel lattice, looking like 'The Welcoming Hand of Singapore'.

Art Science Museum is anchored by a round base in the middle, with ten extensions referred to as "fingers" in the shape of a flower. Each
finger denotes a gallery space sporting skylights at its 'fingers - tips'.
It has 21 gallery spaces covering 64,583 square feet.

Rainwater is harvested and channelled down the centre of the building, flowing through its bowl-shaped roof into a reflecting pond at the lowest level of the building.

The only permanent exhibition in the museum is 'Future World', which was created in collaboration with art collective team Lab.
The museum also hosts progammes, along with exhibitions, including screenings at its Cinema and VR Gallery.

Immediately after the entry, there is a relaxing area of a cafe, where one get a bite ir two.

It was an interesting visit for the 6 of us, which took about a couple of hours of a warm morning.

Visiting the ArtScience Museum was a breathtaking experience! I truly felt like I was transported to another world. The exhibits are incredibly beautiful and immersive, making it a fantastic activity for the whole family. It’s a must-visit spot in Marina Bay if you want to see art come to life!

I visited the ArtScience Museum without booking tickets in advance, and it turned out to be a really enjoyable experience. Even without access to the main galleries, there were still plenty of things to see and do.

One of the highlights was a free film screening, which gave a great introduction to the museum’s unique mix of art, science, and technology. It was both informative and visually engaging.
I also explored several open installations and events located in the basement level, which were accessible without an entry ticket. These areas still captured the creative and futuristic vibe the museum is known for.

Overall, the ArtScience Museum in Marina Bay has a very welcoming and accessible atmosphere. If you’re visiting the Marina Bay area and looking for a cultural experience without planning ahead or buying tickets in advance, this is definitely a great stop to consider.

Omg such a really nice place to visit. Its classy. The building ng has all the luxury stores from a to z worth millions of dollars. Very neatly maintained. Great tourist spot. Highly recommended place for everyone.

I’ve been there many times and I still never get bored. I love the vibe, whenever I visit Singapore, I know I’ll be walking a lot

Marina Bay Waterfront is easily one of the most breathtaking places to visit in Singapore. The moment you arrive, you’re greeted by a stunning panoramic view of the city skyline, with iconic skyscrapers rising elegantly against the sky, especially magical as the sun begins to set and the city lights slowly come alive.

The promenade itself is impeccably clean and well maintained, making it a pleasure to walk along. Every step feels comfortable and safe, whether you’re taking a casual stroll, jogging, or simply enjoying the fresh air. Along the way, you’ll find a wide variety of restaurants and cafés, offering everything from quick bites to fine dining with incredible views.

What truly stands out is how many beautiful photo spots are scattered throughout the area. Whether it’s the skyline reflection on the water, modern architectural landmarks, or perfectly landscaped corners, it’s a paradise for photography lovers.

There’s also a spacious garden area where you can sit back, relax, and soak in the atmosphere.

Overall, Marina Bay Waterfront perfectly blends urban sophistication with natural beauty. It’s a must-visit spot whether you’re a tourist or a local looking for a peaceful yet visually stunning escape

Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade -
A Symphony of Light, Architecture, and Heartfelt Memories: An Unforgettable Experience at Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade

My recent visit to Singapore was filled with incredible sights, but nothing captured the soul of this modern city-state quite like my evenings spent walking along the Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade. It is not merely a path; it is an experience, a masterpiece of urban design, and a testament to Singapore’s vision of blending nature with concrete

. I am writing this with immense gratitude to the planners, maintenance teams, and the city of Singapore for maintaining such a world-class, clean, and awe-inspiring space.

The First Impression: A Grand Promenade
From the moment I stepped onto the wide, impeccably maintained promenade, I was struck by the shear scale and beauty of the area. Stretching 3.5 kilometers around the bay, the promenade serves as a "grand front porch", linking major attractions like the Helix Bridge, Marina Bay Sands, and the Esplanade—Theatres on the Bay.

The attention to detail is remarkable; the flooring is comfortable for long walks, and there is a perfect balance between open spaces and shaded areas to escape the tropical heat.
Breathtaking Views and Architectural Marvels

The panoramic views from the promenade are absolutely surreal. Walking along the water, I felt surrounded by architectural masterpieces. On one side, the iconic Marina Bay Sands hotel towers with its awe-inspiring rooftop Skypark, while on the other, the futuristic dome of the ArtScience Museum resembles a lotus flower opening, perfectly framed by the skyline of the Central Business District.

I highly recommend visiting in the late afternoon, as I did. It allowed me to see the skyline transition from the bright, sunny afternoon into the "golden hour" before dusk, when the city lights begin to shimmer. The way the lights from the skyscrapers reflect on the calm water is a sight I will treasure forever—it’s like a postcard come to life.

The Magic of the Evening: Light, Sound, and Atmosphere
As the sun sets, the promenade transforms into a lively yet tranquil oasis. The atmosphere is vibrant, filled with people laughing, running, and capturing photos. I was particularly impressed by how the atmosphere changes from the bustling, lively sections near the dining options to the more serene, reflective stretches.

A heartfelt thank you to the organizers of the Spectra Light and Water Show. Watching the laser lights and dancing fountains projected to music right in front of the Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands was a highlight of my trip. It is a stunning, free, and accessible experience that showcases the city's commitment to public enjoyment.
A Sensory Experience: Mist Walk and Greenery

The promenade is designed with thoughtful features that make the walk incredibly enjoyable. I specifically loved the Mist Walk—a 250-meter long metal structure that emits a refreshing mist of water. It was a wonderful, creative way to cool down while strolling along the waterfront. Furthermore, the landscaping, with various trees and flowering shrubs, provides a natural feel that softens the surrounding modern skyline.

I visited Marina Bay Sands around golden hour and it was an amazing experience. The warm sunlight on the buildings and the reflection on the bay made the whole place look magical. The skyline view is incredible and the area is perfect for relaxing, walking, and taking photos.

While my wife had her off days, we decided to head out for a jog along the Ulu Pandan Park Connector Dover. Starting from our place in Commonwealth, we made our way to this peaceful and scenic trail and it did not disappoint.

I actually discovered this place earlier during one of my solo mountain bike rides. The path was so enjoyable that I told my wife we had to try jogging here together. And we finally did! The trail stretches all the way to Clementi, making it perfect for long jogs, casual walks, or a smooth cycling session.

Surrounded by greenery and tucked away from the busy roads, it’s truly a great spot to relax, stay active, and enjoy the outdoors. Definitely a place we’ll keep coming back to.

Ulu Pandan Park Connector is a well-loved green corridor in the West, and the Dover stretch — running from Clementi to Ghim Moh and linking to the Rail Corridor — is a pleasant, functional route for walking, running, or cycling. It’s well-paved, spacious, and lined with greenery, making it a reliable choice for daily exercise or weekend outings.

While it’s not as scenic or wildlife-rich as the Faber Hills/Heights section further west (a favourite for birdwatchers), this stretch offers solid connectivity and a strong community vibe. For a complete outing, I recommend parking at Ghim Moh, doing a loop along the connector, and then popping into Ghim Moh Market for a meal — there are plenty of great hawker stalls worth checking out.

I’d rate it a 4 out of 5 — accessible, peaceful, and a practical link between key parts of the city with a rewarding food stop at the end.
Beautiful connector connecting Ghim Moh to the former Jurong Railway Bridge in Sunset Way. It doesn't get overly crowded. A great place for a morning workout.

The Ulu Pandan Park Connector (Dover) is a part of Singapore’s network of park connectors, designed to provide green links for pedestrians and cyclists to explore the city’s natural landscapes. Located near the Dover area, this park connector connects several key locations, including Ulu Pandan Canal, offering a scenic and peaceful route for outdoor activities.

The park connector is surrounded by lush greenery and is home to various species of plants and wildlife, making it a popular spot for nature walks, cycling, and birdwatching. It also provides access to nearby parks, including Dover Forest, where visitors can enjoy the tranquility of the natural surroundings.

The Ulu Pandan Park Connector is well-maintained, with smooth pathways and clear signage, ensuring that visitors can enjoy a safe and enjoyable experience while exploring Singapore’s natural beauty. It’s a great spot for those looking to escape the city’s hustle and bustle while staying active and immersed in nature.
One of the best place in touch with Nature.

This particular Park Connector rans along Sungei Pandan Kechil section of West Coast Road to West Coast Highway.
It's a short 450 metre paved path.
Despite its short length, it's heavily utilised.
Residents living around the estate would walk or jog up and down this short stretch of the Connector in the morning and, in the evening.
The path would be exposed to  full sun in the early morning hrs. And seniors just love walking or do stretching exercises in the sun.

A very pretty park connector that’s perfect for running and cycling, linking West Coast Park with Clementi Woods Park. I often see people jogging here, and with the new HDB and elderly home completed, it’s even more convenient. It connects residents to nearby bus stops, the community centre, and other places easily.

place_name: House on The Hill Mount Sophia - Montessori Pre-School
review_text:
Our experience with House on the Hill Mount Sophia has been truly wonderful. My daughter, Arya, started school here at just 18 months old, and over the past 1.5 years we have seen her absolutely blossom into a confident, sociable, and independent little girl, now close to 3 years old.

This growth is very much thanks to the warm, nurturing environment and the dedicated teachers. We are especially grateful to Ms Hannah, who remained Arya’s teacher throughout her time there, providing consistent care, guidance, and encouragement, along with the rest of the caring teaching team.

The preschool is very well run, with a safe, thoughtfully maintained compound and a rich variety of activities that keep the children engaged and excited to learn. From outings to nature reserves to sports day and art jamming sessions, the experiences offered are meaningful, fun, and developmentally enriching.

We couldn’t have asked for a better start to Arya’s early learning journey, and we highly recommend House on the Hill Mount Sophia to any parents looking for a nurturing and well-rounded preschool environment.

place_name: House on The Hill Mount Sophia - Montessori Pre-School
review_text:
If possible I would rate 6 stars!

House on the Hill has been a wonderful place for my child. The teachers truly care about the children and are very attentive to their emotional needs. Even though my child did not speak English at all in the beginning, it was never a problem.

My child cried and took about two months to fully adapt, but throughout this period, the teachers, the principal, and even the founder were incredibly patient and supportive. They worked closely with us as parents, communicating openly and helping us adjust step by step.

Thanks to their care, understanding, and dedication, my child gradually became comfortable and happy at school. In K2, my child can stay until 6 p.m. and didn’t even want to go home. We are truly grateful for this preschool and highly recommend it to other parents.

place_name: House on The Hill Mount Sophia - Montessori Pre-School
review_text: House on the Hill provide early childhood education programmes focused on holistic learning and development. Their facilities include classrooms and play areas designed to provide a safe and stimulating environment for young children.

place_name: House on The Hill Mount Sophia - Montessori Pre-School
review_text:
We’ve been with House on the Hill Montessori Preschool (Mt Sophia) for many years. One child started around 18 months and graduated at K2, and another is still attending. Overall, it has been a positive and grounded experience.

This is a genuine Montessori environment, not just Montessori in name. The teachers clearly observe children carefully and adjust expectations and pacing based on individual development, rather than forcing everyone through the same track. That personalised approach gave us confidence that our children were being supported appropriately at different stages.

We also appreciated the use of multi-age classes, which are quite rare in Singapore but true to the spirit of Montessori. Younger children naturally learn from the older ones, while older children take on more responsibility and, over time, grow kinder and more mature. It created a calmer and more supportive classroom environment overall.

Several teachers over the years stood out for their warmth, care, and consistency, and strong bonds were formed. Communication with parents was solid, with regular updates, meetings, and responsiveness when questions or concerns came up. At times, the level of parent involvement required for home projects felt a bit heavy, though some families may appreciate that engagement.

One aspect we really appreciated was the food. Meals are prepared on-site by the aunties and are genuinely healthy. There’s a good variety, no salt used, and yet the food is somehow tasty enough even for picky eaters. Snacks are consistently healthy as well, often including items like edamame or a wide range of fresh fruits.

The teachers also go out of their way to create engaging and memorable learning experiences. This includes dressing up and transforming the school for events like book week, as well as planning many excursions to interesting places, especially in nature. These outings are thoughtfully designed, with learning intentionally woven into the experience rather than treated as an add-on.

As with most preschools, there was some teacher turnover over the years. While continuity could always be better, the overall programme quality and philosophy remained consistent.

In terms of fees, House on the Hill sits somewhere in the middle of the range for private preschools. Not inexpensive, but also not positioned as a top-tier luxury option.

Overall, we’ve had a good experience and would recommend it to parents looking for an authentic Montessori setting with caring teachers, healthy routines, and a balanced, holistic approach to early childhood education.

place_name: House on The Hill Mount Sophia - Montessori Pre-School
review_text: Our son attended House on the Hill from 15 to 30 months old, and we were very happy with our choice. From the beginning, the school supported his transition with patience, kindness, and great care. The focus on play, Montessori-based activities, and the warm, welcoming environment are real strengths of the school, helping children build confidence and independence in their daily lives. Parents are also invited to join regular school activities, which gives us a chance to experience our child’s routine and growth up close. We’re deeply grateful to the teachers and staff for their dedication, warmth, and the loving care they gave our son every day.

I absolutely love this school. The facilities are a dream (garden to learn about the nature, pool for water play, parc nearby, ...); the staff is lovely and caring; the curriculum is engaging, diverse and fun. They organise a lot of activities for the kids and involve the parents. I can only recommend it.

Our daughter has been with The Schoolhouse by Mount Emily since she was just 2 years old, and watching her grow in this nurturing environment has been one of the greatest joys of our parenting journey.

Her form teachers, Ms Yiwen and Lang Laoshi, have played a huge role in helping our daughter truly bloom. They have remained a constant thoughout and with their guidance, she now speaks English confidently and beautifully thanks to Ms Yiwen, and her love for Mandarin is all thanks to Lang Laoshi’s fun, engaging lessons. They’ve both made learning a joy.

The school’s play-based curriculum is thoughtful and enriching,  with a focus on emotional development and early STEM activities. Direct access to Mount Emily Park means plenty of outdoor exploration and time in nature, something we deeply value. Each term includes exciting excursions, and the recent Family Day was such a well-run, heartwarming event that brought the whole community together.

We also appreciate how constructive and honest the parent-teacher feedback is during each conference. It’s helped us navigate the different developmental stages with greater understanding and confidence.

A special mention must go to Ms JiaFeng, the hardworking and hands-on principal who always ensures that the children’s well-being comes first.

It will be a bittersweet moment when our daughter graduates from The Schoolhouse. This place has been such a big part of her early years, and we’re endlessly grateful. We cannot recommend it highly enough to any parent seeking a warm, balanced, and nature-rich start for their little one.

Your child will have a fantastic time at Pat's Mount Emily. Our experience with both kids has been incredible. The management and teachers are always welcoming the kids with a heartedly smile and make their best efforts not only to educate and care for the kids but also offer kindness above and beyond their scope of work. The school has many unique facilities that add up to the excitement at school; the veggie garden, sandpit, pool, in-house kitchen, live animal tank etc. You can expect a perfect mix of outdoor and indoor activities for your child that is filled with joy and learning opportunities.
My 3 kids loved the time 🕰️ in school and asked to be in school 🏫 early  indicated the teachers are doing things right.  Many thanks 🙏 teachers 👨‍🏫

We came to Singapore for a short 6 week stay and this was the only place we could find that allowed short stay for our kids (3 and 1) without having PR or work permits. We are incredibly fortunate to have found The Montessori Place of Learning, the admin of getting the kids in was flawless, the price was incredible, and the flexibility was unmatched.

Along with this, the kids had activities from the moment they got dropped off until we picked them up. Lots of arts and crafts, lots of learning, and lots of new skills. My daughter now knows a few words in Mandarin, and her confidence has grown so much in just 6 weeks.

I have no hesitation in giving 5 stars. If I could give more, I would!

If we are ever in the same situation, we would be back in contact immediately.

Note; the address is showing wrong on Google Maps, this is the old location. The new location is in Singapore Shopping Centre.

I consider myself very lucky to have found The Montessori Place of Learning. My 2 year old attended this daycare for 5 weeks and enjoyed it very much. The teachers are affectionate and caring and I could tell that my toddler looked forward to seeing them. I also really appreciated the flexibility of enrolling my child for just the time period that I needed and how easy it was to communicate with Vicky and the teachers. This is a great place for kids!

I fully agree with others regarding this hidden place to learn. To us, it feels like a dayhome, where teachers are reliable and experienced. Most importantly, they kept us updated on our child's progress every day. My child comes home looking forward to going to school the next day. Children are treated with freedom and are given the opportunity to play, explore, and have fun, which is all about - the image of child. Thumbs up!

I sent my 3 year old son here for 3 months. We were very pleased with the at-home atmosphere and the very kind teachers.
1) Flexible
This is a very good childcare center during short stay in Singapore.  Other schools require the expensive enrollment fee or other preparation fee but here set the fee for short term class like us. My son went to school twice a week, 3 hours per a class. We can decide with teachers how many times or how long child goes to school.

(2) International environment
The school mainly speaks English, but they also had Chinese language activities. My son speaks only Japanese, but they accepted him. He seemed anxious at first, but they treated him kindly and I was impressed that he became happy to go school within 3 months. There were 5-6 students in the class depending on the day of the week, and they seemed to come from many different countries. I think it was a good stimulating environment for my son.

(3) Diverse activities
In the daily schedule, Montessori activities are held as soon as he arrived at the school. After that, they sang more seasonally songs, drew pictures, made productions, and did many more activities than I expected.

Thank you teachers!

I am so very glad and grateful that I have found Montessori Place of Learning!

My 2yo child attended this centre for one month (during the holidays of his preschool), and it was a truly enjoyable experience. I liked that the teaching staff were caring and communicative - they updated me regularly on how my child was doing as well as on his activities. I also liked the Montesorri approach and materials. Combined with a relaxed atmosphere and good-sized space, this centre is a hidden gem. Highly recommended!

place_name: My Childhood Place HPGT Studio (Student Care)
review_text: My Childhood Place was introduced to me after many afterschool care centre visits. While there are many premium centres, what sets them apart is their personalised care and a very involved principal to manage the place. You can voice your concerns and needs through Juliana, the principal, and she will promptly reply you. My child's Chinese wasn't that good but Juliana helped her improve by getting her to read the textbook (supervised) everyday. This helps a lot as I have an infant at home and won't be able to concentrate on guiding her with her school work and revisions. When she gets home, we just need to play with her and not think of school work. My Childhood Place also provides nutritious lunch, outdoor park time, and extra activities like art class or holiday programmes. My child really loves it and I would recommend the centre to everyone!

place_name: My Childhood Place HPGT Studio (Student Care)
review_text: My daughter loves the center and we cannot recommend it enough. The center is clean and spacious and the teachers are caring and responsive. It has made the transition from kindergarten to primary school so much easier for both my child and us. Highly recommend it if you are looking for a student care in River Valley.

place_name: My Childhood Place HPGT Studio (Student Care)
review_text: Very spacious, clean and teachers are thoughtful. I chose this pace because they have enough time for study and play since my child is still lower primary, it’s more balanced to spend time after school. Only concern is the place is very cold but teacher will mention kids to wear jacket or drink warm water.  I appreciate their communication with parents are very well too.

place_name: My Childhood Place HPGT Studio (Student Care)
review_text: Initially was upset that the school inhouse student care rejected us but that brought us to MCP and we felt that is a much better choice. We love the big open space and bright environment. My son love attending student care so much that when we pass by on school closure days he will want to go up. They send teachers to pick the kids from the primary school and give us picture update for the first week so that we get peace of mind. Spelling and ting xie are tested and corrections are done in SC. No more vomiting of blood to try teach him ourselves. They will also help buy assessment books for him to do in SC as P1 and P2 not much homework. The teachers are attentive and noted my son is weaker than his peers in chinese and offered tuition (at additional cost). Interesting art classes are also available at additional cost. These are of course optional but save us so much time and effort to bring him ourselves on the weekends.

place_name: My Childhood Place HPGT Studio (Student Care)
review_text: Great place to let your kids be at after school. School work would be completed and spelling and 听写prepared for the week. Teachers are quite affable and responsible. Principal of the centre is a kind and friendly lady who would do her best to help within reasonable means. Trustworthy.

I had a very good learning experience at PSB Academy. All the campuses are modern, clean, and well-equipped. The staff and lecturers are very supportive and helpful. I especially liked the friendly environment and the variety of programs available. I can confidently say that this is a good place to develop your skills and build a successful future career.
I especially enjoyed the IBS module and the RSSC module because Dr. Melissa Liow was my lecturer. She helped us kindly and explained everything clearly.

I was a student in Dr. Melissa Liow's Global Marketing and Marketing Strategy classes. Throughout my studies at PSB Academy in collaboration with Coventry University, Dr. Melissa Liow provided invaluable guidance, encouragement, and industry-relevant insights that greatly enhanced my learning experience. Her engaging teaching approach and willingness to support students helped me develop a deeper understanding of marketing concepts and strategic thinking. As a working adult balancing full-time employment and part-time studies, I especially appreciated her patience, constructive feedback, and dedication to helping students succeed. The knowledge and skills gained from her classes have strengthened both my academic development and professional growth. I am grateful for her mentorship and would highly recommend her as a lecturer.

Dr Salitha is genuinely one of the best lecturers I’ve had. Her teaching is clear, engaging, and highly professional. She explains concepts in a way that makes them easy to understand and relevant to real-world application. Her standards are high, but that pushes us to perform better. It’s clear she is deeply knowledgeable and committed to her students’ success and wellbeing. Anyone would be lucky to have her as a lecturer
Dr Salitha Nair is a wonderful lecturer. She explains concepts clearly and guides students with great dedication. I truly appreciate and am very grateful to her!

Thank you Dr. Tamilchelvi for all your help throughout my whole journey in Coventry University. From Tax module, Audit, Corporate Financial Management, and to the completion of my final project, your encouragement, constructive feedback, and high academic standards have shaped not only my work but also my confidence as a student. Your patience and willingness to guide me through challenges have made a lasting impact on my academic development. I am truly grateful for your mentorship and dedication, which have helped me grow both as a person and professionally.

I would like to commend Mr Ton Kiat and Ms Grace for their excellent service. They were patient, attentive, and highly knowledgeable in addressing my concerns. They took their time to explain everything clearly and relayed my concerns.

They were both professional and friendly. I truly appreciate their help and would highly recommend their service :)

Nanyang Polytechnic has a balanced mix of modern and slightly older buildings, but everything is spaced out nicely so the campus doesn’t feel packed. The school is big but not overwhelmingly huge. NYP has a very “technical” atmosphere — you see a lot of labs, training centres, and workshops dedicated to engineering, IT, media, health sciences, and business.

The student life feels active but not overly noisy. There are always events going on in the atriums, and you’ll often see student ambassadors or clubs promoting activities. The sports facilities are strong too, and the school has a reputation for doing well in certain competitions.

What I liked:

Spacious campus with a good mix of study areas, labs, and event spaces.

Very practical training environment — you feel like you’re preparing for real industry work.

Bus and MRT access is convenient for most people.

Facilities feel genuinely useful and hands-on.

What could be better:

Some buildings look old from the outside.

Certain blocks can be hard to navigate at first.

Food courts get busy really fast during lunch.

Overall:
NYP is a very grounded and practical poly. Good facilities, good balance, not too flashy but reliable and well-run.

Polytechnic that I love. With infocomm live science health engineering design and business. Free wifi. Great greeneries for caring and nurturing study environment. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for students teacher tourists Expats and locals. Educational excellent for future leaders.

Came here for a sharing session. Impressed with the facilities and cleanliness. Student designs are very interesting. Someone ever said. The toilets revealed how civilised a place can be. Here in the design school I saw toys in the toilet, and different kind of hand washes in the toilet. How to interpret that, I wonder.

Very clean and friendly school. The staff and cleaners there r all friendly and kind. The course is digital marketing was very useful for me and I got a job successfully after graduation. I didn't get the chance to study on campus due to covid, but I took the course online by NYP and visit the campus after covid measurement is allowed.  I am proud to be one of the graduates from NYP.

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

Singapore Management University (SMU) is a top-notch institution that's making waves in Asia! Established in 2000, it's a public university located in the heart of Singapore, offering a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs.

*Key Highlights:*

- *Rankings*: SMU is ranked #3 in Singapore and #1016 globally, with top rankings in fields like Investment Banking, Real Estate, and Accounting.
- *Research*: The university is known for its high-impact research, with faculty members collaborating with international researchers and partners in the business community.
- *Campus*: SMU's city campus is modern and well-equipped, providing students with a vibrant learning environment.
- *Programs*: SMU offers bachelors, masters, and PhD programs in various fields, including Business, Economics, Law, and Computing.¹ ²
*Student Life:*
- *Student Body*: Over 12,000 students from diverse backgrounds.
- *Facilities*: State-of-the-art library, sports facilities, and administrative services.
*Admissions:*
- *Tuition Fees*: SGD 11,500 for local students and SGD 24,600 for international students.
- *Scholarships*: Available for deserving students.
Overall, SMU is a great choice for students looking for a world-class education in a vibrant city-state. Would you like more info on SMU's programs or admissions?

Open house. SMU is situated in central part of Singapore, mainly around Bras Basah-City Hall district. A reputable university offering undergraduate studies in Law, Economics,Accountancy,Business, Computer Science, post graduate and doctoral courses. Well connected to nearby mrt and public transportation networks, easily accessible to students traveling by public transport. SMU consists of 10 buildings with 6 buildings are schools/academic, 4 buildings are campus / student facilities, spread across the campus city area.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent learning experience with strong industry relevance

SMU Academy provides a well-structured and professionally run learning environment. From onboarding to programme briefings, everything has been smooth, organised, and clearly designed for working professionals.

What impressed me most is how industry-relevant the content is. The modules are practical, up-to-date, and focused on real application instead of just theory. The facilitators are experienced practitioners who encourage discussion, critical thinking, and sharing of perspectives — making each session engaging and meaningful.

Administrative support has been responsive, and the networking opportunities are a big bonus, bringing together participants from different industries to exchange ideas.

Overall, SMU Academy delivers a high-quality learning experience that truly adds value for mid-career professionals and business leaders looking to upskill and stay ahead. Highly recommended.

I came for open house today, hv an email that claims by showing I can get a goodie bag. The woman (student) at 3f collection q starting point on sun 2.30pm was arrogant and unwelcoming. I showed my email but she say it is not, and use her body to block me, and offering no further help, full stop.

Unwelcoming sounding and arrogant enough for an open house. I walked away and refused (forgo) the goodie bag. No big deal. Just a goodie bag, no need to destroy your school's reputation.

Unwelcoming attitude by student like this during a open house tells a lot about the school.
Coming from the IE exchange program has completely changed my perspective and has open new opportunities. Highly recommend to expand you network and net worth.

place_name: The Pique Lab | PSLE, O Level Science & Math Tuition Centre
review_text: Before attending the P5 CCI Science Course, my child was struggling with open ended questions. The beautiful coloured and detailed notes were well received by my daughter. Mr. Iman explained well. The workshop made her more interested in the subject.

place_name: The Pique Lab | PSLE, O Level Science & Math Tuition Centre
review_text: Really thankful to TPL for helping my kids enjoy Science and build a strong foundation from young. The colour notes for each chapter are super helpful in summarising key points, so revision is much easier. Starting from P3 really gave them confidence in Science, and that made a big difference for them.

place_name: The Pique Lab | PSLE, O Level Science & Math Tuition Centre
review_text: I have been with the Pique Lab for about 3 years now. All the teachers were amazing at explaining each science concept meticulously and clearly, however, Ms Vivien is a teacher that stands out the most. Ms Vivien is always cheerful and ready to welcome students with a smile. She patiently explains science concepts using real- life examples, stories and always reminds her students to use keywords in their answers. I am very grateful for Ms Vivien’s patience, humour, wit and guidance for 2 years. Ms Vivien will also take time after class to explain questions outside of class to her students, going through every step with nothing but patience, effort and dedication to her work. My science results have drastically improved under her guidance. Thank you Ms Vivien for making every Pique Lab lesson interesting, fruitful, engaging and enjoyable! Time always felt like it pasts so fast during the lessons. I would have never gotten where I am today without you! 😊

place_name: The Pique Lab | PSLE, O Level Science & Math Tuition Centre
review_text: My daughter shared that Ms. Joanna shared a lot of stories during the P6 CCI Science Course, and made the lessons interesting and engaging. She also taught them the method to answer OEQs.

place_name: The Pique Lab | PSLE, O Level Science & Math Tuition Centre
review_text: We think that the S1 CCI Chemistry Course was very useful for me because it helped me to understand the concepts more effectively as well as learn the answering techniques of science questions. Ms. Siew Qi also made the lesson very fun and engaging! I really enjoyed the course and I would definitely recommend this course to other parents!

This MRT Station has three MRT lines running through it - the Circle Line, the North South Line and the North East Line. For me, Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station is a convenient MRT Station from which I can alight to go to Plaza Singapura with which it is so "seamlessly" connected underground and with an exit leading to basement level two of Plaza Singapura. Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station is also a convenient MRT Station from which one can alight to visit The Istana on the days that is open to the public.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station is one of the most important and well-connected MRT stations in Singapore. It connects multiple lines, making travel across the city extremely convenient and efficient.
The station is clean, spacious, and well-organized despite being very busy. Clear signage, multiple exits, and easy access to malls like Plaza Singapura make it very user-friendly for both locals and tourists.
I often pass through this station when heading for city attractions booked via HolidaySG.com, and it’s a perfect central hub to start exploring Singapore.
Great connectivity, excellent facilities, and very well maintained. Highly recommended for smooth travel around Singapore!

Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station (NE6) is one of the busiest and most convenient transport hubs in Singapore, serving as a major interchange that connects the North-East Line, North-South Line, and Circle Line.

Despite the high traffic, the station is well designed with clear signage, wide passageways, and multiple exits that link directly to shopping malls like Plaza Singapura, nearby offices, and cultural spots.

The station is clean, well maintained, and has good accessibility features for those with mobility needs. A vital connection point that makes travelling around Singapore smooth and hassle-free.

🏷️ :
#DhobyGhautMRT #NE6 #SingaporeMRT #PublicTransportSG #TravelSingapore #MRTInterchange #CityTravelSG #ExploreSingapore
