Dhoby Ghaut MRT station is one of the most convenient and well-designed transport hubs in Singapore. As an interchange for three major MRT lines (North-South, North-East, and Circle Lines), it provides seamless connectivity to different parts of the island.

The station is clean, spacious, and well-lit, making it a pleasant experience to navigate even during peak hours. Signage is clear, and there are escalators and elevators for easy access. The variety of exits also leads to key landmarks like Plaza Singapura, Fort Canning Park, and other nearby attractions.

What I love most is the convenience of having a mall directly above (Plaza Singapura), where you can grab a meal, shop, or even catch a movie before heading home. It’s a station that truly blends utility and accessibility.

Dhoby Ghaut is one of the most vibrant and bustling transport and lifestyle hubs in Singapore! As a major interchange station, it connects multiple MRT lines seamlessly, making it incredibly convenient to travel anywhere. Beyond transit, the area features great shopping malls like Plaza Singapura, excellent dining options, and beautiful green spaces like Fort Canning Park nearby. It perfectly blends urban convenience with local heritage, making it a fantastic spot to meet up with friends, shop, or just take a lively evening stroll.

Personally quite like the fact that this is probably one of the better maintained busstops in the entire Orchard Road stretch. Shelters all through from the train station, and shopping malls, are a huge plus for my transit experience. The well maintained bus guides and transit timings, although not entirely accurate, is very helpful in allowing me to make public transit arrangements.

The bus stop has cover all the way to the MRT station. Even raining, you won’t get wet. There are so many bus lines available here to get you to many different places. The bus stop also has a signage showing the buses arrival times. This is super awesome when you know how long you have to wait. The bus stop is long enough for two buses to stop at the same time.
Definitely a weird flow. One of the few if not the only bus stop that stops on the right side of the main road
This is a very convenient bus stop for changing from one bus service to another, or MRT to bus or vice-versa.
It is very near Singapore President Mr Lee and he is my inspiration

If you frequent the Plaza Singapura or Dhoby Ghaut area, this bus stop likely stands out as quite iconic. It's notably long and spacious, offers excellent shelter, and provides crucial connectivity for many using the Purple NEL line to get into town. I particularly appreciate that people seem to maintain the cleanliness of this bus shelter. The significant connectivity that has been developed here over the years is also remarkable.

a very useful bus stop that not only provides sheltered pathways to dhoby ghaut mrt, but also plaza singapura! this bus stop services 24 bus lines, providing the ultimate convenience. this is definitely a useful bus stop!
Transfers between the RED and PURPLE transit lines are quite a walk. But as far as mega interchanges go, it's pretty decent. The attached malls also make it a convenient meeting spot.

Its a public space with having lots of office and workpaces as well. So the area work as an meetup junction for all the people who works around the area. It has markets one of them is plaza singapura. The basement is connected to the dhoby ghaut stn. It has recreational parks. The park seems so natural that the nature loves will enjoy the place. The park names  fort canning park is a historical place as well.

Experienced some inconvenience this evening at the North East Line because the gantry linking to basement 2 of Plaza Singapura doesn't allow people to exit, only for entry. So I had to walk a big round to get to Plaza Singapura.

At the southern end of the MRT North-South Line, this is the station that connects you to the Marina South Pier for ferries to the southern islands of Singapore and Bintan and Batam Islands in Indonesia. It is also the connecting station for the seaside promenade where you can catch spectacular views of the bustling Singapore port or where you can charter a fishing boat.

The station itself is beautifully decorated with a very interesting installation of tiles engraved with pictures and artwork.

Sinay says:

The station has only very limited facilities so if you need food, drinks and snacks items, please head to the South Pier.

Like everywhere in Singapore, the MRT Station is spotlessly clean.

Love how they added mirrors. Really meaningful initiative for dancers to use, really wish they can add these Mirror Moves to other MRT stations in Singapore especially some TEL stations, where space is a plenty
Female toilet so dirty!! So so so many cockroaches. Really unacceptable given it is a MRT and right near cruise centre.

the best way access to the city affordable budget friendly for seafarers.This railway station has a mission that automatically dispenses juice for two dollars This is a good place for sailors to relax after shopping and heading back to the ship.

This is the southern-most terminal station of the North-Sputh line. Its exits lead to the Marina South Pier ( exit B) and the Marina Cruise Centre (exit A), making it very convenient for visitors to the southern islands and the regional Cruise Centre. There are sheltered walkways linking the exits to either the pier or the cruise centre.

We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.
My favorite hawker center in SG. The flavors are amazing and priced reasonably. After walking around Chinatown this is the best place to fill your tummy with something local yet affordable meals!

A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

Review of Kopitiam Food Hall @ Plaza Singapura

Kopitiam Food Hall at Plaza Singapura underwent a renovation in 2021, and the facelift gave the entire space a fresh yet nostalgic identity. The layout was completely revamped, with décor inspired by 1950s kopitiam culture. A standout feature is the large mural along the exterior wall, painted with scenes reminiscent of our grandparents’ era, alongside the display of old-school items and crockery from that time. These thoughtful touches help evoke fond memories of the past and reflect Kopitiam’s long-standing place in Singapore’s food heritage.

Here’s what I’ve tried so far:

1. Kim Dae Bak Korean – ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (4.0 stars)
In my opinion, this stall leans more towards a fusion of Japanese and Korean cuisine. The menu is extensive, featuring many hotplate-style dishes such as teppanyaki omu rice, donburi, honbap, ramyun, bento sets, and bibimbap.
My usual choice is their bento value set, which includes salmon and teriyaki chicken, offering good variety and value for money. That said, the Japanese rice didn’t quite meet my expectations, which slightly affected the overall experience.

2. Ju Bao Xuan – ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (4.0 stars)
I ordered the grilled fish and added luncheon meat. The fish was grilled nicely, with good texture and flavour, and the soup was rich and satisfying. However, I was expecting more vegetables beneath the fish, but there were only a small amount, which made the dish feel a little less balanced.

Overall Impression
The renovated Kopitiam Food Hall offers a pleasant dining environment that blends nostalgia with modern convenience. While individual stalls have their highs and lows, the overall concept, atmosphere, and variety make it a comfortable and familiar spot for a casual meal in town.
dirty table, food was just ok. Hawker chan rice with dry roasted duck with mostly bone part and no manner cashier and cooker. drink station also bad manners worker.

Clean, plus aircon. Pricier then our usual kopitiam.

Ban mian is mid for more expensive. 3/5

Pepper lunch is same as always, awesome. 5/5

Dumpling ay the entrance, wantons r bad, jiaozi/gyoza is super gd. Noodles soup and dry r pretty mid, but they give alot. 3/5

The manager here is really friendly and hardworking. Several times when I come to have dinner, I've seen the long hair young manager on duty. Every time she sees my family and me, she smiles and nods with us. Several times I've seen her walking and observing food court, picking up litter on the floor, and she's politely asking the cleaning staff to help wipe the tables. This is very commendable, she maintains and showcases the company image of Kopitiam.
Btw , nasi lemak ayam taliwang is my first choice, drumstick very crispy and juicy! No wonder has won Michelin stars for many years.

Bought apple & celery juice from Kopitiam at Plaza Singapura. The taste was quite bad—didn’t feel fresh and the flavor combination didn’t work well. Definitely not worth the $4.60. Hope they can improve the quality...this happened to me 2nd time

This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

This is a long established shopping mall very popular with the locals especially when Yaohan, a Japanese brand first opened up it’s flagship super supermarket here and it was always crowded with families visiting, shopping and dining. It’s sitting immediately on the right when you face the ISTANA.
Here for yummy lunch at muji cafe and shopping at wiggle wiggle (popup till 4 Jan 2026). Almost feel like in Japan and Korea all in 2 hours.

A cosy, quiet cafe spot off Dhoby Ghaut, and a nice place to catch up with friends, my friend and I were here until they closed for the day 😅 Service was pretty good as well.

Chicken Katsu Sando (S$15.5) - loaded sando, came with 2 pieces of chicken katsu and layered with lettuce, teriyaki and Japanese mayo. Enjoyed the chicken katsu which wasn't too greasy or soggy, the bread slices were a bit sweet as they were toasted with butter and honey, making for an interesting and slightly sweeter sando. Relatively filling and pretty tasty!

Creamy Tamago Sando (S$12.5) - my friend liked this so much that she returned to get this again. A great veg option and she thought that despite the simple flavours, this had the right balance of egg and mayo. Even though the portion looked a bit small, this was very filling for her. The bread was toasted quite heavily with butter and honey, but she didn’t mind that.

Misu Latte (S$8.9) - the latte here was quite strong and slightly bitter, neither was it too milky. The misu drinks were topped with cream and powder (cocoa or matcha), making for a nicely indulgent drink which is different from your usual lattes. Overall the drink wasn’t really sweet, but had a good amount of indulgence too.

Misu Matcha Latte (S$9.9) - my friend thought this wasn’t too sweet, and it also wasn’t bitter.

For more food reviews, check out my IG @foodlesstravelled!

📍 9Toast Cafe @ Dhoby Ghaut, Singapore

While waiting for our Japan visa appointment nearby, we decided to stop by for a quick coffee break—and it turned out to be a convenient little spot 👍

🥤 Drinks we tried:
🍋 Iced yuzu tea – refreshing and just right, not too sweet
🍵 Iced matcha latte – smooth and nicely balanced
🍓 Strawberry matcha latte – light, refreshing, and not too heavy

The place gets quite busy, especially during peak hours, but service is fast so the queue moves quickly. Seating is a bit tight, so it’s more of a quick recharge stop rather than somewhere to sit and relax for long.

✨ Overall: A fuss-free and practical place for a quick drink break if you’re in the area

Went back for a second time and I was so confused by the inconsistency because this time round the bread was barely toasted, did not taste sweet at all and just tasted like normal white bread!!

—
Sando is pretty good but cafe looks quite beaten up and dirty for a place that’s so new. While I was dining the aircon above me was dripping too. Also, although the staff had their hair tied up, their hair were still hanging everywhere which I thought was quite unhygienic. I’ll be back but hope the hygiene improves

Love how their toasts are honey glazed so it gives it a sweet and slightly crunchy exterior which goes super well with the savoury fillings.

The new misu series drinks were super good too. The latte and matcha were a little muted but I feel like it’s so that the drink doesn’t taste too jelak when paired with the thick, rich, savoury cream. Really feels like a dessert drink.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Plaza Singapura)
review_text: The food here is always exceptionally delicious, which is why we find ourselves returning to the Waterway Point outlet time and again. Every visit feels like a treat, with flavors that never fail to impress. The quality remains outstanding each dish is prepared with care and attention to detail. Whether we dine in or opt for delivery, the experience is consistently excellent. The team maintains high standards across the board, from presentation to taste. Truly a reliable favorite for our family!

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Plaza Singapura)
review_text:
I had big expectations seeing that it had 4.7 stars on google reviews but was pretty disappointed.
Pros:
Staff were friendly and helpful and space was large enough to accommodate big groups and families. Portions were decent. Price point was in line with quality of food. I guess we pay for what we get.
Cons:
For me, the food quality level is just a notch above Saizeriya. Everything we had was mediocre. The roasted cauliflower were dry and not tender. Sautéed mushrooms were bland. Pizza had too much cheap mozz cheese that was hard to swallow. Even the clam pasta had that same mozz cheese which hardened and became a chewable clump as a topping. We ordered loads but they all did not impress. Ok, maybe the fries… the fries were crispy.

This place is popular for families and young kids prob because of the value but not if you are actually looking for proper italian food.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Plaza Singapura)
review_text:
I had a really great dining experience here. The staff member who served me, Hakim, was especially attentive and professional. He took the initiative to ask if I had any food allergies before I even placed my order, which I really appreciated — it made me feel well taken care of.

The food was delicious, not oily at all, and felt well-balanced and healthy. You can tell they put thought into both taste and nutrition. Overall, great service and great food — I’ll definitely be coming back again.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Plaza Singapura)
review_text: It's my first visit at this ps outlet. The pan fried seabass is very fresh and tasty without any fishy smell! I like the toast Ciabatta which I dun usually see it on a standard menu.. very appetising. Overall the food is not oily and light. Will come often..🤤

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Plaza Singapura)
review_text: Been here for several times already and still one of my favorite restaurant. Food is always great. Love the ambiance too. Staff are very friendly specially Mitz. She ensure that we have an incredible dining experence. :)

It has been years since I last came here. Decided to bring my colleagues and recommend them since we came here for my boss birthday today, 26022026. My boss was served a complimentary bowl of the birthday noodles 🙏
Never regret, kudos to them since we made the booking of 9 pax at 11.30am sitting in one the VIP rooms. We ordered one of the set meal plus some ala carte. Nice food, the atmosphere and the great service of the 2 female staffs especially the one wearing spectacles (top bad I didn't catch hold of her name).
She was very patience, giving good recommendations of the food, kept changing our plates & topping up our cup of tea also.
Credit goes to her 👏👏👏
We had a wonderful lunch and we really enjoyed ourselves. I am glad to hear that my colleagues kept giving thumbs up on the food, saying they will bring their families and friends back here.
Thank you so much 🙏🙏🙏

It was ok, not great. Not 5 stars for sure. Maybe a 3.5. The pork belly glutinous rice was very soft. But everything else was a little lacking in taste, maybe not as salty as I would like it to be. Prefer the duck at DXE instead. The place is authentically decorated but omg, the music played was not great at all, was some opera type, which made me want to leave the restaurant quickly.

A place we keep coming back to whenever we crave proper Nanjing-style comfort food. The duck oil buns remain consistently good — fragrant, soft and incredibly satisfying. Their Yang Chun noodles are another must-order for us every single visit; simple but done exceptionally well.

The rest of the dishes were equally delicious, portions were generous, and food arrived surprisingly fast even during busy hours. Service staff were warm, attentive and efficient throughout the meal. The ambience is comfortable and welcoming too, making the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

One of the more reliable Chinese restaurants in Singapore that still delivers both quality and consistency. Highly recommended.

Had dinner here and again lunch on the following day. My favourite is definitely the duck+pork xiaolong bao. Dont be put off by the price, cos it is super big. I like the jicai and pork wrapped in tofu skin, as well as the veg shaomai. The palace chicken soaked in pickled chilli interesting. Abit sour abit sweet, and the entire bowl is filled with chicken.

By 630pm on a Thursday, it is 50%-60% filled, so be early if you want to avoid dinner crowd.

They are famous for their duck dishes and it did not disappoint. I ordered the roasted duck and duck dumplings which are delicious. Their brown sugar rice cakes are quite unique too. Food is good and I don’t mind to come back again.

I had a wonderful time visiting Dame Museum Café with my friends! The space is absolutely beautiful and spacious, making it a great place to sit back and relax. They have a very nice selection of food, and the prices are highly reasonable. Most importantly, the coffee is great!

As a bonus, Fort Canning Park is located right behind the café, so we took a lovely walk there. It's such a beautiful area. I will definitely be visiting more often!

I had a wonderful experience at Dame Museum Café. The layout of the space is beautifully designed—it feels open, modern, and perfectly complements the museum atmosphere. It’s an ideal spot to relax and decompress after walking through the galleries.

The food was equally impressive. Everything was fresh, well-presented, and delicious. Whether you're looking for a quick bite or a proper meal, the quality here is top-notch.

Highly recommend stopping by if you’re visiting the museum or just looking for a serene place with great aesthetics and even better food!

Bright n cooling environment. Very very good ventilation, fully aircon. A very nice feeling. The place is clean.

Food was fantastic 😋 enjoyed my big breakfast. Good bacon n sausage. The Bolognese taste great meat were fresh.
B4 i know it my wife finished her pasta =_="(must be good)😅

As usual i do not have high hope for coffee made by normal restaurant. My flat white tasted too strong for coffee(lack of milk). Anyway i still like the coffee cuz the coffee beans were fresh.

Pray the museum doesnt hike the rental.
2 thumbs up.

I think this is the best chicken burger I tried. Its so juicy and crispy!! I also like the touch of flavor of lemon grass.

The side of fries is so amazing. Crispy, crunchy, perfect pair with the chicken burger.

The place wasnt intimidating, its cozy and clean, its not crowded although there are several people, the area is wide enough for you to chill and enjoy your food. This is a great way to end my trip in SG. I’ll def come back.

We love dining here, whether or not we’re visiting the museum. The prices are reasonable, and the ambience is lovely, with natural sunlight streaming through the glass roof. It’s a relaxing spot—perfect for gatherings or taking a break. The kids especially enjoy it, including the kid-friendly menu. Overall, it’s a great place suitable for all ages.

Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

I was here recently and went up to the highest level to explore. Seems like they have a good place for someone to hold a small seminar here but in a public place that is quite exposed to other sounds. But useful nevertheless. Not seen in this short video but here.

While waiting for appointment time for lunch and meeting up with friends on a Thursday afternoon, I shot this video.

I was here today (11 JANUARY 2026) to shop for a few items at this CS Fresh or Cold Storage supermarket at basement level two of Plaza Singapura. I have shopped here on many occasions from when it was previously occupying a bigger footprint than currently, but I am glad that it is still a "big enough" supermarket with a super wide variety of products and that it is still very well stocked. The only "complaint" that I have - and this is not a serious complaint - is that some sections of this supermarket is freezing cold.

I had a really unpleasant experience at Cold Storage Plaza Singapura recently on 18 April.

I made a purchase that included perishable food and had already paid for a plastic bag since I needed it to store my leftovers later. After completing payment at the self checkout, I approached the cashier to request the bag since this outlet does not provide self service bags unlike other supermarkets.

When I asked for the bag, a female staff member came over and questioned whether I had actually paid for it. I told her I had, otherwise I would not be asking. She then said she would not know because they had received emails about customers not paying for bags. That response felt very inappropriate and accusatory. It made it seem like I was being suspected of dishonesty over a five cent item. I even told her she could verify through the POS system if she had doubts.

Instead of resolving it calmly, the situation dragged on with unnecessary back and forth over something so trivial.

To be fair, the outlet itself is well stocked with good food and a great selection. However, the lack of staff presence at the self checkout and the way this situation was handled made the overall shopping experience frustrating. I hope management can improve staff training and customer service in handling such situations more professionally.

I kind of miss the presence of Cold Storage near my area on the north. CS's price is normally higher, but they have good range of unique products that are hard to find in other supermarkets, especially baking products.

It was a delight when I found a range of dog treats available there where you can try bits and pieces of everything especially because my dog is picky. It's too bad that he only likes one type, but for other dogs, go for them!

Huge spacious supermarket at the basement of plaza Singapura. Service of staff was better. Waited for staff to finish washing the rack before turning to assist me. I tried to call out to her but no avail. Luckily, another staff saw me waiting and called out to her. Cashier wasn’t in the best mood, but at least she tried to help me pack the stuff into my grocery bag.

I really like how Cold Storage has revamped many of their outlets by now. This CS Fresh outlet is no different. Located on B2 of Plaza Singapura, you're able to get some fresh food items, beyond all the usual fresh groceries you'd find at a supermarket. Really liked that they have quite an extensive cheese section as well, in case you need to grab some for your cheeseboard or a party.

Eventually got myself some duck rice for lunch, and you really can't complain about the quality nor the quantity, for 5SGD.

The ambience was warm, cosy, and perfect for the season—especially with the Christmas carols playing softly in the background. It really added to the overall festive experience.

We tried the 300g Wagyu sirloin steak ($46.98), and it was absolutely worth it. The meat was tender, flavourful, and cooked beautifully. I especially loved the crispy garlic slices on top—they added the perfect touch. Truly delicious.

The truffle mushroom burger was decent, though not particularly memorable, but the sea salt fries that came with it were surprisingly good and stood out more than the burger itself.

The carbonara pasta was alright—creamy and comforting enough, though it could use a stronger flavour to make it more satisfying.

Overall, a lovely place with a great atmosphere and a couple of standout dishes. Would return again for the steak alone!

Big supermarket run by NTUC Finest at Clarke Quay. They are located around 2 mins from the Clarke Quay taxi stand, along River Valley Road.

They have a range of fresh produce, cooking essentials, meats, cheeses, frozen goods, alcohol and also tourist souvenirs.

What’s interesting is the food court where there is a variety of cuisines to choose from. A stall even allows you to buy the meats from the supermarket and they will prepare it for you to eat on the spot!

A bar in the supermarket also allow you to order drinks by the glass.

A real outstanding find! 💖💖💖

This grocery store is full of surprises, with an amazing collection of food stalls—including a charming pub-style corner serving fresh oysters. The selection of beers and wines is incredible, both in variety and price.

I tried the Italian section, ATavola, and had a fantastic pasta. I loved it so much that I came back the very next morning for breakfast.

Totally loved it. Totally recommended! 💎

What a find at Clarke Quay!
Tucked inside the FairPrice Finest, there's a surprisingly spacious and comfortable cafeteria area where you can order snacks and hot meals. What really sets it apart is the option to enjoy beer and wine at supermarket prices—just a small service fee added, making it significantly cheaper than your average bar or restaurant in the area.

It's a perfect spot to take a break, especially in the mid-afternoon when it's quieter.
Whether you're looking to cool down with a cold drink or have a casual bite, this place offers great value in a relaxed setting.
Highly recommended if you're in the area and want to unwind without the usual Clarke Quay price tag.
Nice place to chill and relax with friends. Huge selection of wine and cheese. For vegetarian, there are fries and vegetarian prata.

This 7-Eleven outlet at the basement level of Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station is a "tiny" one, bordering on being described in my opinion a kiosk - with a lot more width than length. That said it appears to be a popular place for MRT passengers to just buy and go with it selling perhaps products that are customized to such customers. Hence, one might even say that it is an essential amenity in Dhoby Ghaut MRT Station.

I would like to raise a concern regarding the recent experience at this store around 8pm on 19 Mar. The staff appeared to be exercising their authority in an unreasonable manner.
While purchasing cigarettes, my friend was asked to present identification, even though he is clearly a mid-age man . Furthermore, the staff refused to accept Singpass as a valid form of identification and insisted on seeing the original physical ID.
I would like to understand whether this policy is in line with your company guidelines. The situation felt excessive and inconvenient, and I hope you can clarify if such requirements are indeed necessary.

I went here to buy a bottle of Dasani. When I took it out of the refrigerator it tumbled to the ground accidentally but it was not a high impact drop. I paid for the drink, and was holding it in my armpit when I noticed my entire sleeve was wet. I assumed it was condensation but it kept leaking and I realised there were pen marks on the bottom of the bottle. Someone must have tampered with the bottle and put it back. I went to the lady working there to ask if I could take a new bottle. She mumbled something in English which I didn't understand, but before I could clarify she went ahead to chitchat with her friend behind the counter. Then I approached her with my two other friends who helped me explain to her again and showed her the pen marks. She then replied in Chinese, "这是应为你掉了。” I was extremely frustrated as the water was coming out through small holes and there were pen marks hence it was not a result of me accidentally dropping it, yet she was so rude and unhelpful. Instead of letting me take a new one, she told me to just pour the water into my water bottle and didn't let me take a new one. Whole time face was very unpleasant. Please improve your staff training and customer service. I do not know the name of the staff member but this incident happened on 19 March 2026 around 5:45 PM. I am disappointed as I had already paid for my water yet was not allowed to just take a non-damaged one. I hope you will reflect on this case of horrible customer service.

Regarding to your employee Linda, she didn't even know the product details. I bought a pack of these egg and she say the packaging never stated that the egg can be heat? Then she heat the egg for 10 second cuz I insist. Rediculous, the packaging mention about heat 50 sec. Then I show her the packaging did states the heating time, and she like don't care and says the juice will spilled if she heats. Excuse me is this how you service ur customer or she don't even have the knowledge to serve the product? But neither way is bad, please trained your employee.

The Sat morning Malay staff got attitude problem. Always showing bad face. I requested to heat up my item with the specific time. She didn’t listen and shorten the time. When I asked her to heat up again coz it’s still cold then she’s not happy somemore.
Good cashier Kind and welcoming got a momentum card that worth 7-15$
One of the bigger footprint 7-eleven shops around. A larger variety of foods in many categories from chips to refrigerated drinks, to ready to eat. Super friendly staff at this outlet.
the staff keeps leaving every 5 minutes and doesn’t return even after 5 minutes? i have been waiting here for around 10 minutes and there is no cashier at all
super friendly staff !
Nice facilities, tables, chairs can sit and eat there but charge station was not working! Need to fix otherwise 5 stars ;)
It’s very small, came on 2:30pm on Saturday. Maybe 12-15 booths only. Selection is very limited. It’s better to go luggage market on Sunday. The rental here for 1 booth is so ex.

Could be interesting... Price range from $2 - $10+... Really is a thrift second hand items (clothes/ accessories etc)... Just a stretch along Dhoby Ghaut Xchange... Sunday full of Philippino workers...

For people who wants to book/booth:

Great place to clear your clothes! If you just wanna clear your closet and can price your stuff for as low as $2; I highly recommend this place! most of the buyers are actually willing to spend, high footfall, air conditioned!! We were here for 2 days and we actually earned some $$$ out of the stuff we no longer use/need.

So if you just wanna clear and get rid of your stuff 10/10 would be recommend you book!
Nothing to see!

It's a small outlet yes but it's not too cramped as compared to outlets like J9, Chong Pang, or Khoo Teck Puat. The best part of this outlet are unique leg machines like the glutes builder. I love chilling at outdoor areas. They should consider having class at the lawn area.

The not so good part: Only 2 half rack, no lifting platform, and the shower area are cramped. There is only 1 hook in the shower cubicle so it's a hassle go change. You have to use the toilets outside.

Overall, still a good place to train (unless you're doing powerlifting or weightlifting). It's located on the third floor. The mall is quite dead but there are plenty of eateries around (if you like Chinese food).
Great experience here, friendly service, clean and new equipment. Nice and bright space with an amazing balcony

Small and cosy AF located on the top floor of GRID mall. Unique thing is that they use Precor machines which I generally prefer over Life Fitness. Nice that they have a T-bar row and also a couple of leg equipments that I've never seen anywhere else from Precor's glutebuilder range. Leg press seemed a bit confusing to setup but felt pretty solid after figuring it out.

Overall a very new and fresh gym that has the bare essentials to get a good workout in with some unique pieces for legs.

Pros: Updated Precor machines and wide variety of equipment, including plate-loaded machines for back and glute etc - is that a smith-like hip thrust machine? Definitely a solid gym IF it’s not peak hour.
There’s also an outdoor area with an ice bath (which is not functioning yet?)

Cons: space still too small, especially squeezy near the dumbbell area. Some parts of the layout is awkward - when using the chest fly machine, the elevated floor padding is just 1 step to your right and opening your legs a bit means having to step on the elevated padding. Due to space constraint, couldn’t fit more benches at the dumbbell area, and the lifting platform and chest supported t-bar row is right next to one of the bench. Unfortunately they can’t expand the gym to use some of the outdoor area as well 😅

Anyways good attempt to maximise the space, although I feel some things can be shifted around!

The yoga classes are honestly the highlight for me. They run on Mondays and Fridays and are held outside on the lawn, which makes them super relaxing and a nice change from typical gym classes. The instructors are great too.

Everything feels new, the equipment is great, and it’s always clean.

Only small issue is the changing rooms don’t have hooks, so it can be a bit awkward when changing.

Overall, very solid place to work out.

I have been going to the GymPod that is located directly outside Dhoby Ghaut Xchange frequently.

The location is extremely convenient and is located within the MRT. The interior is also one of the more spacious interiors of the other branches. The equipment available is decent, and I also liked that there is a really big mirror and an exercise ball.

However, during my more recent visits, I realised that some of the equipment, like the hooks, seems to be coming loose. Multiple times, the equipment was also not placed back properly by previous users (e.g., exercise mat in the middle of the pod; bench dragged out and also left in the middle of the pod). This has happened so frequently, and even after documenting them with pictures and timestamps, and providing feedback to the management, not a lot has been done. I also noticed on multiple occasions that there are banana peels and other food and drink wrappers thrown in the dustpan. Clearly, not a lot of reinforcement has been done to ensure that the space is kept clean and tidy by other users.

Overall, I would recommend this particular branch of GymPod due to its spacious interior and convenient location.

It's okay. Good location and decent range of stuff. Only problem if you want to train a bit heavier the weights are fairly limited. You can realistically go up to 4 plates at most. The bench is not very good but tolerable and does have a decent swell for your shoulders although the rack is close to the wall so it's hard to place the bench well. The power rack is narrow and has big feet so if you are tall or have a wide squat stance watch out for that. It does have bands which is nice. Price is good. Can change in the nearby bathroom.
The place not bad, i gym with my group of friends. We booked the entire gym for our weights circuit training. Ha! 😆
Love this space!!

My 3 kids “graduated” from swim lessons with AQZOG swim school with Coach Raymond at Delta pool. we only started in September 2024, in less than 2 years. No choice..nobody to bring them to pool, sigh, temporary pause.... But at least from novices who didn’t know how to swim, (2 of them didn’t even dare jump into the pool), all could swim already. The basic strokes, survival strokes… Can have fun in the middle of deep pool comfortably… good enough. In fact they skipped direct to test Swimsafer stage 2 (

Pool facilities and infrastructure are pretty good with 1 Olympic lap pool and 1 teaching pool. Toilets are mostly clean with around 12 showers (Men) and for an entry price of $1 or monthly passes for $10, it is a pool accessible to the public. The staff who man the entrance/exit are friendly and greet you when you enter/exit and there’s 3-4 lifeguards observing the pools constantly.

The people using the pool on the other hand leave much to be desired. Between the Mr Magoo lookalike who walks across lanes at the end of the Olympic pool instead of using the teaching pool, the people who see a busy lane and insist on not just joining that lane but not swimming around the people already in the pool, the old people who cut the queues at the entrance when the pool first opens and the kids doing swimming lessons who can start in lane 2 and swim diagonally to lane 4 in a single lap, you would think that half the users are either blind/vision impaired or have no social awareness

Also, do note that the pool crowd website is not accurate for use as the pool had around 40 people in it when the website showed 13% full. 100% occupancy would thus be around 300 people (~38 people per lane), this means the pools would be practically unusable at 30%+ capacity as you’d be dodging and weaving around people every lap you swim.

Before deciding to swim here do take note that the pools get crowded and the people who use it are not the most civic minded with minimal social awareness.

Very decent, neatly maintained community pool. Toilets are clean and it has separate changing rooms for kids and adults.

As like any other place, based on the crowd and people your experience might vary, as there will be some folks who would be abusing or misusing everything. Similarly can get too crowded during weekends and there will be too many coaching classes on-going.

3 different pools, one for toddlers and young ones. One lap pool and a pool seperate for learning.
Great pool and facilities. Whoever put in the floors in the Men’s changing room did a bad job, water collects in puddles. I will be back for another swim soon.

Lived in Redhill for many years and have witnessed the gym, pool, running track undergo improvements. Kudos to the relevant authorities for upgrading the facilities here. The entire complex was torn down & rebuilt. It reopened in 2023. It even has pickle / padel ball courts now. Bonus for getting a MacDonald’s in.

Fantastic swimmming facility and friendly staff. It’s also cheap so no excuse not to use it!

I swam at the competition pool and it was mind blowing how beautiful and peaceful it is. They allow paddles, fins, etc no pull buoys or boards like most swimming facilities here so come prepared. Lockers are free and rely on setting your own code.

Competition pool schedule is smaller than training pool so be aware of this.

It’s near the mall so easy to load up on delicious food after swim!

I spent some time swimming in the pool—50 meters of peace and quiet. The pool was super clean, and the safety guards were on top of everything, keeping an eye out for swimmers. The staff were all incredibly friendly and helpful, which made the whole experience smooth.

The changing room was spotless, and the lockers use combination locks, so no need for coins or tokens.

I was there for about 90 minutes, and at most, there were two people in my lane, so there is plenty of space to swim. The pool's covered, so no risk of sunburn, which is a bonus. The water temperature is on the cooler side, perfect for those coming from colder places—locals sometimes wear wetsuits, but for anyone from a chillier climate, it’s just right.

The OCBC Aquatics Competition Pool stands out as the top pool in Singapore, offering superior water quality compared to other public pools with barely any chlorine smell. Additionally, the female changing rooms closer to the competition pool are impressively spacious, equipped with ample lockers and shower rooms. A major highlight is the availability of hot showers, enhancing the overall experience. However, the hair dryers could see some improvement, but considering the reasonable cost of less than $3, it’s not a significant issue.

Training Pool -1.35-2.0m
Competition Pool -3m
World-class Aquatic Centre which was an excellent venue for hosting the current World Aquatics Championships 2025!
Good sport spot. Unfirtunately Singaporean not visiting sports games even if it is a world championship.

I came to GV Plaza for the “5 centimeters per second” movie marathon. The movies were great but I wanted to rave about the kind staff at the popcorn counter.

I was buying popcorn before the movie marathon and the kind auntie staff saw that I didn’t have free hands to hold the movie posters, my hoodie, the popcorn and drinks with 2 hands so she offered me a rubber band and a plastic bag to hold my movie posters.

It’s a small action from the staff but I just wanted to express my appreciation for the kind & thoughtful gesture - Thank you! ✨

I only come for the movie screening exclusive because of Twice: One In A Mill10n. I love how they give the Twice movie poster after making a movie ticket purchased! It was a great movie experience I've ever had!
Food is okay but theatre 6 is damn musky & smelly, which affects my appetite. Review as per all the other older reviews. Recommend the theatre to do frequent upholstery cleaning on their seats.

Golden Village Plaza Singapura offers a premium cinema experience with modern facilities, comfortable seating, and a diverse film selection spanning Hollywood blockbusters and Asian releases. The sleek design, featuring signature red and gold accents, complemented by the convenient location within a popular mall, makes it a go-to destination for Singapore's movie enthusiasts.

The counter staff in this centre provides one of the best service to seniors. They are knowlegeable n direct you to the right person to get the job done. Each time when I m at Lavender area , I will drop by the centre if I need any help and the staff has treated me with promptness n gave me the information / guidance needed. Keep up the excellent service as I m aware this area has the most low educated seniors that need patience in handling. One can see from the attendance of seniors patronising the centre's activities. It is built on trust n the centre is being promoted by words of mouth from the residents living there.

Walking to this location and found Jalan Besar CC and find it very quiet and pleasant place. Had my vaccination Appointments booked for this CC. Vaccination processing for me is straight forward, ⏩,easy and smoothly taken. Less than 7 to ten minutes.
The pick locker is beside the drop off point, outdoor here

5 stars to Jalan Besar Community Club! I would like to specifically thank Mr Tony Tan and his colleague. A kind gentleman had returned my wallet to Jalan Besar Community Centre, and both Mr Tony Tan and his colleague proactively and persistently reached out to me via my mobile phone to inform me of this. Mr Tony Tan was highly professional and carefully went through the full protocol with me to verify that I was the rightful owner of the wallet. Throughout the process, he treated me with great kindness and courtesy.

Surprisingly, parking was a breeze, curbside parking right in front of the CC! Must be because of P2HA. Took my daughter for her Covid vaccination. 1st day for school kids and just noticed a few school kids coming for their vaccination. The process was another breeze. Very smooth and efficient check-in. My girl was out in 30 minutes. Kudos to the staff for their courtesy and efficiency.

Istana Park is lined with lovely plants and flowers along its walkways, yet surprisingly few people stop to wander through and enjoy the scenery.
Hardly any locals or tourists seem to notice this beautiful expanse of greenery and blooms.
Hopefully, with the planned redevelopment, the park will become more inviting and help revitalize the area, adding fresh life to Orchard Road.
Easy walkway with beautiful bougainvillea over the head cover. Very nice park with busy road along opposite isatana house..

Istana Park is a small but pleasant green space right at the beginning of Orchard Road. It offers a welcome break from the busy shopping district and feels surprisingly calm given its central location.
The park is clean, well maintained and perfect for a short walk, a quick rest or simply enjoying some greenery between meetings or shopping. The open lawns and shady trees make it a comfortable place to pause and reset.
While it is not a large destination park, its value lies in its convenience and tranquillity in the middle of the city. A nice little urban escape in central Singapore.

At night, this is how the Festival Arch at the Istana Park looks like. According to the wikipedia page, the design is to "to evoke a feeling of formality and grandeur".

The arch is a four-storey-tall (26m long) concrete and stainless steel structure, inspired by the gateposts and railings of the actual Istana entrance, symbolising the park's role as a gateway to Singapore's Civic District.

At the base of the arch is a shallow rectangular "reflecting pool" (40 cm deep) where we can see the arch's reflection, seems aiming to create the impression of an urban oasis

Park around it is very peaceful. Only thing I've never been able to figure out, is why no one is ever in this park, almost. I think it's the feeling of constantly being watched. Not sure if it's my imagination, but Ive always thought it felt like the panopticon down there, even before the proliferation of cc TVs as the cost went down. Maybe that's why the cafes which set up next to this landmark always seem to be doomed. There's a pleasant visual arts center here a little further out.

You'd be surprised, but the single row of plants thoroughly shutters up the field of ambient noise from dhoby ghaut. Great advertisement for the silencing power of vegetation.

The park is a charming and well designed green space located right in the heart of Singapore’s Central Business District. Surrounded by major streets, the park offers a peaceful contrast to the city’s fast pace.

Positioned in front of the Presidential Residence, the Istana, the park spans 13,000 square metres and serves as the gateway to the Civic District Trail an initiative that highlights Singapore’s historical and cultural heritage.

The main attraction is the 26 metre high Festival Arch, a striking stainless steel and concrete structure set in a reflecting pool. Inspired by the Istana’s gateposts, the arch stands as a symbolic entrance to Orchard Road. The nighttime lighting adds a tranquil and elegant atmosphere to the space.

The addition of the Istana Heritage Gallery in 2017 adds an educational layer to the visit. While the park is relatively small, it is beautifully landscaped and ideal for a short, reflective walk or a quiet break in the city.

Stepping into this urban oasis adjacent to Dhoby Ghaut MRT is like hitting a slow-motion button on city life. The moment you enter, it's like walking into a verdant sanctuary. Thanks to its dense, vibrant greens, this park offers a blissful refuge from the tropical sun, making it the perfect spot for a tranquil escape. It's truly a peaceful surprise right in the heart of the city!

This is the open air area of the busy Mass Rapid Train (MRT) interchange.

Dhoby Ghaut has 3 lines: Circle Line, North-South Line, and North East Line.

People can gather here enjoying the bustling city view overlooking Plaza Singapura and Istana (the presidential palace).

There is the Konfrontasi memorial besides the small garden.

There is an art facility here where exhibitions and classrooms are held.

During day time, can spot the chicken family: the cocok, 2 hens, and a bunch of chicks.

Small park area with an art facility to stop by. There are swings for up to four people to enjoy under the trees. Watch for wildlife on the lawn, rooster and other animals seem to frequent the area. To the east side is a small but colorful flower garden area.

A small but convenient  park to visit in the middle of the city of Singapore. There are a small number of swings that create a refreshing breeze when set in motion. Yesterday, the night sky looked spectacular as the moon shone through the branches and leaves.
It isn't much of a park, per se, but it is very green and is a great place for interacting with other people.

What an oasis right by Nassim Hill Gate entrance at the Botanical Gardens.

Gorgeous place for locals and tourists alike

Uncountable species of orchids to view and explore.

Indoors air-conditioned and mist houses available to shield from the heat too!

What’s more local seniors is $1 entry or free entrance on some occasions.

Highly recommended 🐦‍🔥🌺

Please come enjoy and promote this wonderful gem!!!

A Must-See Botanical Masterpiece: The Singapore Orchid Garden

The National Orchid Garden in Singapore's Botanic Gardens stands out as an absolutely essential stop for any visitor, even those with limited time. We were completely enthralled by its beauty, quickly naming it one of the most beautiful places we have ever visited.

It is important to note upfront that entry requires a fee, which for us came to 15 SGD per person. However, this cost is well worth it for the experience.

Upon entering, visitors are greeted by a choice of several walking paths winding through the meticulously curated landscape. The orchids and other flowering plants are not merely displayed; they are beautifully and thoughtfully arranged, creating stunning visual compositions at every turn. The atmosphere is serene and engaging, with vibrant colors and fascinating shapes.

Beyond the main paths, there are several small gazebos and other structures where plants and flowers are arranged to maximize their aesthetic impact. These quiet corners offer a chance to pause and appreciate the detailed work that goes into maintaining the garden. We also appreciated the many plaques and signs available, which provide detailed information about the diverse flora you find there, adding an educational layer if you have time to stop and read.

Unfortunately, we didn't have too much time to spend here—a fact we regretted the moment we left. But even with limited time, we would highly recommend a visit to the National Orchid Garden. It is a stunning showcase of nature's artistry.

Brought my parents for a walk. No crowd no wait buying tickets on the spot. The flowers looks great and multiple photo spots. Takes about 2 hours to cover the entire garden with stops at photo spots.

Great for a walk during good weather. You can make a stop at sembcorp house zone to escape the heat.

Even though the flowers are planted and not native, the garden is easy to navigate and lush. The air conditioned section for cooler climate orchids was much appreciated in the extreme humid heat. A lot of information texts help you learn a lot new about the plants.

Incredibly beautiful and well mantained garden. It's not free but it's worth every penny and more. It's much more than orchids and much more than I expected. It's hard to describe it in a few words. The pictures will tell you much more.
A much cleaner park than I remembered it was 20 years back. It’s really windy till I developed a running nose in the mid afternoon. Great place to chill as not so crowded like east coast park.
Quite nice place. Just the beach needs a bit of cleaning. Saw people fishing and wind surfing with sails. And even a bird catch fish. Not to mention airplanes taking off from Changi airport.

A nice place to be quiet, reflect, and be with loved ones, bustling with children and people cycling. There are also people fishing, selling things, everything is orderly and nice, not far from Changi Village when I was here, ill come back here in the future.

A 7am visit to Changi Beach Park offers a perfectly serene and quiet stroll, feeling like a well-kept secret from the bustling city. As the sun begins to rise, casting a soft, golden glow over the sand and sea, the park is enveloped in a peaceful silence. The only early birds are the dedicated park cleaners, whose quiet work in the morning calm adds to the tranquil atmosphere rather than disturbs it.

The true magic, however, is the unique soundscape. The tranquility is gently broken every few minutes by the deep, distant rumble of aircraft—a powerful yet strangely soothing reminder of the world in motion as planes make their final descent into the nearby Changi International Airport. It’s a captivating contrast that makes this simple morning walk feel both secluded and connected to something larger.

My family's favourite beach. Lovely sun, sand, sea and sometimes jellyfish. Lol..
Nice long stretch of beach to the roundabout point for that Instagramable picture with the finger.

Toilet amenities are OK. If you're hungry for bites, changi village hawker centre is only 10minutes walk away. Parking on weekends can be very limited due to the trending wingfoiling sport.

And oh.. not supposed to tie anything to trees now. No hammocks unless self standing.

Wah the only time i felt very proud to be Singaporean n heartfelt impressed 😁, compared to other local museum.

Spacious n air felt good with well control good input of cool air. Was very happy our local senior citizens who attended n usher people around. Love the old uncle vibe🫡 pointing out the rules n urge us to follow it.

Alot of information n good read around the Museum. 2 thumbs up to put in effort to let kids enjoy the visit also.

Sunday very hard to find carpark.. was lucky.. heng heng someone drove off from fort canning carpark. YMCA parking alot too little LOTs.

Anyway really enjoyed the visit. Keep it UP! 🥰🥰

I learned a lot about Singapore’s history through the museum’s interactive multimedia exhibits and high-quality visuals, which made the experience feel immersive and engaging. From its early days as a fishing village to its rise as a global metropolis, you really get a sense of the hard work and vision that shaped Singapore into what it is today.

What I especially appreciated was how values-driven the museum felt. During our visit, there was an activity where Singaporeans were encouraged to write heartfelt letters to expatriates, and there were also several exhibits showing what life was like for expats working in Singapore and the kinds of jobs they held. Older generations were also given space to share their stories with younger visitors, which added a meaningful intergenerational element to the experience.

Everything felt centered on connection, empathy, and understanding, and that was the theme I loved most about the museum.

The building itself is also beautiful, with plenty of photo spots throughout, so it’s easy to take nice pictures to share with friends.

The only caveat is that the Singapore Odyssea exhibition is a little pricey, so we ended up visiting the free option, Once Upon a Tide, instead. Even so, it was still well worth seeing.

A great stop if you’re looking for something educational, meaningful, and family-friendly.

National Museum of Singapore, located at Stamford Road, is the oldest museum in Singapore and one of the largest in Asia. Catch the history of heritage preserved in the majestic building with exhibits of paintings, sculptures and archaeological finds.  Ticketed entry to the museum. While you are there, do explore the Singapore Odyssea: A Journey Through Time, which is free for local citizens and PRs. Pick up a wrist band at the entrance and be accompanied with your pet avatar alongside the interactive exhibition shows and videos. Museum visit is no longer dry but interestly fun and engaging for all. Highly recommended!

This National Museum of Singapore is very worth to visit!
Noted that most of the exhibit's explanation is in English. And most of local friends are chinese educated.

It would be better if there is something (i.e. QR code) for other foreign visitors to scan and it automatically translate to their preferred languages to read. I believed Singapore has many of this IT talents who are able to go this impressive things.

The museum is incredibly interactive with AI-powered exhibits that really bring Singapore’s history to life in a fun and modern way. The displays are engaging, and the helpful museum staff were always around to assist with any questions or issues, super friendly and knowledgeable. The place is spotlessly clean and the air-conditioning keeps everything comfortably cool, which is perfect in Singapore’s heat. Definitely a great spot for families and tourists who want more than just looking at old artifacts.

The only downsides were the roller mouse on some interactive displays that didn’t work as smoothly as expected, causing a bit of frustration. Also, the food options at the café and the souvenir shop felt quite limited, more variety in local snacks or unique souvenirs would have been nice.

Overall though, it’s still a top-notch museum experience and I’d recommend it to anyone visiting Singapore.

Waterfront Promenade is exactly what you hope it will be — a relaxed, scenic walkway along Marina Bay that’s perfect for a leisurely walk, people-watching, or taking in the city’s iconic skyline.
We started our walk in the late afternoon and stayed until after sunset. The path is wide and well-maintained, with plenty of places to pause and enjoy the views of Marina Bay Sands, the Singapore Flyer, and the sparkling lights of the city reflected in the water. There are plenty of benches and shaded spots, which made it easy to take a break and soak up the atmosphere.
What really stood out was how peaceful it felt even when there were other people around. It’s busy enough to feel lively, but never too crowded to enjoy your own space. There are food and drink options nearby if you want a refreshment, but we also saw many locals and visitors bringing their own snacks and just enjoying the waterfront vibe.
The promenade is great at any time of day — sunny mornings, golden hour before dusk, or in the evening when the city lights come on. Whether you’re a visitor exploring Marina Bay or a local out for a casual walk, it’s a beautiful, easy-going stretch that captures that quintessential Singapore waterfront experience.
Highly recommended for a relaxed outing and especially good for families, couples, or anyone who loves great views with minimal fuss.

The Waterfront Promenade is a peaceful escape in the middle of the city. With its wide walkways, calming sea breeze, and stunning skyline views, it’s perfect for slow walks, quiet reflection, or late-night strolls. The atmosphere feels cinematic—especially at sunset and at night when the city lights reflect on the water.
It’s not crowded like other tourist spots, which makes it ideal for solo time, journaling, or simply letting your thoughts wander. Whether you’re healing, thinking, or just enjoying your own company, the promenade offers a calm, grounding energy that stays with you even after you leave.

🌃✨ Strolling along here at night felt like walking into a postcard! The skyline lit up, the reflections on the water, and the cool breeze made it such a dreamy spot. Totally worth the walk and definitely one of those must-do experiences when you’re in the city!

One of my absolute favorite spots in Singapore to simply relax and enjoy the moment. You get an amazing view of the Marina, and it’s the perfect place to slow down and take in the city. There’s often live music or street performances, and in the evening you can watch the lights of the business district slowly turn on — such a beautiful and calming sight.

A wonderful place to unwind, people-watch, and soak in the Singapore vibes.

The Waterfront Promenade in Singapore is a vibrant blend of urban design and natural beauty, offering visitors a scenic space perfect for leisure and relaxation. Stretching along Marina Bay, it provides panoramic views of the city skyline and iconic landmarks like the Marina Bay Sands and ArtScience Museum. The pathway is ideal for joggers, cyclists, and those looking to take leisurely strolls, especially at sunset when the bay is bathed in warm hues. The promenade’s highlights include artistic installations and lookout points that enhance the experience, while food and drink options nearby cater to different tastes. In the evening, the space comes alive with lights reflecting off the water and occasional events or light shows that add to the allure. Overall, the Waterfront Promenade is a must-visit for both tourists and locals seeking a refreshing, open-air experience amidst Singapore’s bustling cityscape.

Not much of a park here. It’s basically a long pavement running alongside the Pelton Canal — more of a functional connector than a place to linger. No benches, no garden rest stops, no shade; just a straight walkway & cycling track linking Paya Lebar, MacPherson & Aljunied.

This place is very good to visit and it is very quiet. In the early morning and in the evening you can come here for biking, jogging or just relaxing walks. There is also a canal connecting from the Kalang river, where you can fish and see the wildlife found in this river such as herons, storks and if you are lucky you will see a family of wild otters looking for fish in the canal.

Nothing special, not much landscape works, this is a bare minimum PCN but conveniently connects you from MacPherson area to downtown Kallang.

If you are cycling 🚴‍♀️ you will have to push your bicycle over the pedestrian bridge over PIE

A quiet place to go, kallang river has rich biodiversity, various fishes, monitor lizard, great heron, sometimes kingfisher can be seen. Walking, cycling, jogging, up to you. The PCN here is very close to central area,  thus I suggest go by bicycle to enjoy the night scene at marina bay, feel the cooling wind, you will have a good sleep.
All nice and good until.... You reach this bridge. At least they built the little blue path for Cyclists to roll up your bicycle.

Kallang Park Connector is an absolute gem for anyone who appreciates a bit of wilderness in the heart of the city. As a regular spot for jogging, it offers much more than just a paved path; the air is incredibly fresh, making those morning or evening runs feel revitalizing. What really sets this route apart, though, is the incredible biodiversity. During my visits, I’ve encountered everything from vibrant insects to the occasional snakes in the river—nature is definitely thriving here.

For those who find themselves constantly looking toward the water, the sightings of various fish species are a fantastic bonus that adds to the charm of the area. It’s the perfect blend of a functional fitness route and a mini-safari. Whether you're working on your cardio or just looking to clear your head, the connection to nature here is top-notch. If you're looking for an all-in-one spot to stay active and enjoy the outdoors, this is the place to be. Highly recommended for nature enthusiasts and fitness lovers alike!

Kallang Park Connector is a linear green trail in Singapore’s Park Connector Network (PCN) that runs along the Kallang River and links Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park in the north to Kallang Riverside Park in the south.

It’s about 7.87 km long and provides a scenic route for walking, jogging, and cycling through several central residential areas

We definitely enjoyed our walk along the Kallang PCN.

There's a weird sign pointing towards bishan park (the wrong way), confused us for a moment.

One of the most scenic park connector in Singapore linking Clark Quay and City center to Kallang, Kallang Bahru, Potong Pasir and all the way to Bishan Park. Perfect for cycling, jogging and brisk walking.
Very good area for relax your mind

The 2 stretches of Kallang Park connector that go under the bridges are finally open this week! Lovely artwork. Kudos to the artists that put them up and pat on the back for the workers whp were involved in the construction.  Joggers and cyclists can now bypass 2 traffic lights at Kallang Bahru and Upper Boon Keng Road, to get from Bendemeer Road to Kallang MRT. Celebration!

This place displays and shows the history of this canning fort from the 90s to now. The place was not big but was a nice place for history lovers chilling here.

I came to this place when Singapore Night Festival, so I'm not sure if I need a ticket or not.

1pax = complimentary (SGNF)

The exhibition is quite interesting. With quite a lot of different sections to look at. Not very big. It’s free to enter.
But the customer service officer at the help desk is not very helpful and a little curt, not so welcoming. Possibly a more social customer facing person should have been selected to man the desk.

Excellent place, which takes you through the history of Singapore from its founding as Singapura "Lion City" in 1299 through to present day. The chronology as you start the exhibition details all the key events in the country's history. As an Englishman, I was particularly interested in the impact that Sir Stamford Raffles had when, under an agreement signed in 1819, he secured substantial control of the territory. Lots of information available and well presented. You learn how brutal the Japanese occupation was when they took control in 1942. I've included a lot of the information boards in my photo attachments for those who wish to learn more.

Visited the Fort Canning Heritage Gallery, a place steeped in history and significance. Walking through the exhibits, I was transported back in time to understand how Fort Canning Hill has played such a central role in Singapore’s journey—from being the seat of ancient Malay royalty to its pivotal role during WWII.

The gallery’s displays of archaeological finds, historical artefacts, and interactive exhibits were incredibly engaging. I found myself reflecting on the layers of history that this hill holds—how it has witnessed power, war, and transformation over centuries.

Exploring the gallery was more than just a history lesson; it was a reminder of how much we can learn from the past. Surrounded by the tranquil greenery of Fort Canning Park, it felt like the perfect blend of nature and heritage.

If you’re in Singapore and haven’t been here yet, I’d highly recommend a visit. It’s a place where stories of the past come alive, leaving you with a deeper appreciation of this island's rich legacy.

After spending some time under the scorching hot sun, we were happy to find shelter in the Fort Canning Centre (air conditioned environment). This is where this Fort Canning Heritage Gallery is located.

Here we were introduced to the historical aspects of the area, including the Fort Gate, a former Christian cemetery, and artifacts dating back to the 1300s.

It is free admission, wheelchair accessible and public toilets available. Worth the visit if you are visiting Fort Canning Park.

I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

We had a great time at Fort Canning Park, especially at the children's playground area. The kids really enjoyed the swings and slides, and the playground was well designed for young children.
There was also a wash basin nearby, making it easy to clean up after playing. The facilities were convenient and family-friendly. The location is very close to Fort Canning MRT stop.
The park itself is spacious, green, and well maintained, offering a nice escape from the city. Unfortunately, we didn't have enough time to explore all areas of the park, so we definitely plan to return on our next visit to Singapore.
Overall, Fort Canning Park is a wonderful destination for families with children who want to enjoy outdoor play, nature, and a relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommended for a few hours of family fun

Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

Enjoyed a sunny but hot walk around the park to take in its history and also Raffles Garden. Some good views and lots of plants and trees to admire. The main draw for us though was the heritage centre which contains a fair bit of military history relating to Singapore.

We’ve been with this school for more than two years and have had a very positive experience. What first attracted us was the lovely outdoor playground and weekly activities like mud kitchen and water play. The learning is well-rounded and age-appropriate, but what we value most is the warm and caring atmosphere. The teachers and students share a very close bond, and our child feels truly happy and supported here.

A special thanks to Ms Nichelle for her patience, care, and dedication — she has made a big difference in our child’s journey.

From the very first visit me and my husband made 5 years ago, we knew this was the right preschool for our son — the outdoor space is so big and full of nature exposure, exactly what we were looking for. Now both our son and daughter love going to school every day. The teachers are kind and caring, and we can really feel the love and patience they give to every child. We’re so grateful to have found this wonderful place. 💕
Now my son is graduating this year, and we will truly miss the warm teachers, the joyful environment, and all the beautiful memories he made here. We are so grateful for this wonderful journey and so happy we have chose TSHWC. 💖

The teachers and the overall staff are so welcoming and warm.  They are patient and very caring and really made settling in very easy.

They also patently tell you all details daily of the child’s activities.

Overall this Pats has a fabulous campus with great play areas etc. .

Our son has the most enjoyable years of his preschool days at The Schoolhouse. He loves the outdoor space and the playground. Teacher Nichelle who followed him since playgroup days have been termed his princess and role model in school. Thank you to all teachers who took care of him

My little girl started with this preschool since her Nursery days and is now in K2.  It is bittersweet that she will soon leave this wonderful school.  My husband and I were first attracted to this location due to its spacious grounds and emphasis on play.  My little daughter has been very happy here, being nurtured by teachers and administrators in her various academic and creative endeavors.  We love how the school has various cultural and enrichment activities beyond the classroom.  The goal is always to develop the whole child.  My little daughter will certainly miss this school; it has given her a wonderful start in life.

CJC gave me some of the best years of my schooling life—shaped by lifelong friendships and a deeply values-driven community. What has stayed with me most is how we were seen fully, not just as students, but as people growing through both clarity and confusion.
