Nearly twenty years on, my memories of my time here still remain a safe place I return to when life gets complicated.

To my juniors: cherish your time here, make more friends, and hold on to them. This is the place to find your future village.

Just graduated from here and i’m proud to say that i’m a CJCian. Had the best time of my teenage life here! I made so many connections and good friends here! If i had the choice to turn back time, I would always still would choose to come to CJC again.

Poor school, school is very stingy about money so they rarely host fun events. They only care about student leaders who get more opportunities like trips and camps. As such there is no level camp for the ordinary student.

Effectively NO AIRCON,
The school only offers 25% of the student population aircon home rooms and the aircon is like 15year old, sometimes it's not even cold. This means that j1s are literally walking into hell. (Imagine having lessons to 5pm at ~38°C)
While j2s have to alternate and share aircon classrooms per semester.

If you have just enough points to come this school, you'll have a more fruitful life at tmjc.
Easy to access. Staff helpful, but slow. Expressway construction in progress.
Friendships are bonded over 30 years ago and we are back to celebrate CJC 50th!

place_name: Irwin's Study - GP Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
My learning experience at Irwin’s Study has been extremely enriching and meaningful.

The lessons were always engaging and productive. Mr Irwin is a dedicated and patient tutor who explains concepts thoroughly and clearly. He is always willing to help and goes the extra mile to ensure we understand the material and improve steadily.

The lessons focused on building strong comprehension skills through consistent practice, helping me to understand texts more deeply and answer questions with greater confidence and precision. The essay outlines provided were also especially helpful as they broadened my worldview and trained me to think critically about complex issues from multiple perspectives. They not only improved my writing structure but also challenged me to develop more mature and thoughtful arguments.

Overall, my experience at Irwin’s Study has greatly improved my skills in GP and made me a more confident and reflective learner, with skills that can be applied beyond the classroom.

place_name: Irwin's Study - GP Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
attending gp lessons with mr irwin made a huge difference in my A-Levels journey! Before joining, I was struggling to grasp key skills - but with his guidance I not only improved significantly, I ended up with an A (from a D) for a levels! there was a good balance of skills and content taught for p1 and p2, which enabled us to become well-rounded gp learners. I always felt supported, challenged and encouraged, and the lessons were engaging, very interesting, planned and conducted with care.

gp became more manageable and fun to study with mr irwin's help :) thank you

place_name: Irwin's Study - GP Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: Attending tution lessons on Tuesdays were something I looked forward too as I really find myself learning a lot from Mr Irwin's lessons. I love that the classes included both paper 1 and paper 2 skills, as well as real world content and examples to beef up our content bank. Mr Irwin really grabs the attention of the whole class despite having a big lesson size, and is always very willing to aid in any GP-related work, be it self-initiated or school work. The ample practice Irwin's study has given me helped me improve steadily from a D to a B, and I am super grateful for that!

place_name: Irwin's Study - GP Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: Tuition lessons conducted by Mr Irwin were extremely easy to understand and included a lot of useful resources. Mr Irwin was very welcoming and responsive to questions during lesson time as well. Overall, lessons were enjoyable and easy to follow along :)

place_name: Irwin's Study - GP Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
Very engaging class, Mr Irwin takes the time and effort to ensure everyone gets a chance answering questions.

The lesson is structured so that you can brush up on both paper 1 and paper 2 skills at the same time!

Classes are super fun and I looked forward to it every week.

There are two exits here. Air-conditioning very strong. Super clean MRT but couldn't easily find the toilet signage.

The attached photo is at exit A.

Fort Channing MRT station has two exits, each leading to different areas, however close. It was relatively empty when I arrived there, and it was quite extensive inside.

In terms of cleanliness in the toilets, it is the usual clean standards, which you don't have to worry about. If you are going to the Fort Canning Park, exit via so that you can reach there faster. Overall, quite a nice MRT station.
quiet mrt station. good aircon always. toilets clean. staff nice and smiley. easy to navigate. close to clarke quay
Woah the mrt staff is so nice once my phone died and I didn’t bring my EZ-Link and he let me ride the MRT for Free!!Love this staff
clean but security only does checking of Indian individual bags eventhough so many other people are carrying big bag packs…just seems so wrong leh

- Located near Fort Canning Park, this is a beautiful, spacious, air-conditioned area that's easy to get to. However, the journey from the station to the park is quite long, while the return trip is surprisingly close. From this station, you can also take a train to Expo Station to transfer to the CG2 train to get to the airport. It's perfect for those who enjoy exploring places like Chinatown or strolling through the park.
Great, wherever it is near
convenient
Right at the escalator to go up hill
There are also many rental bicycles at the bus stop just outside Fort Canning Station Exit B.
Mosquitoes all around. Every time got bitten

Ordered the chicken breast pasta and the chicken burger. Unfortunately, both dishes lacked flavor and didn’t stand out. Service was also quite limited during our visit despite the place not being busy. The space itself is cute and inviting, but overall I felt the quality of the food and experience didn’t justify the price point.

Beautiful hidden gem in Fort Canning. Saw it once while on a walk, went back to try it. Loved the peaceful garden vibes, spring summer ambience, and cozy atmosphere. Perfect spot for a relaxing brunch or quiet catch-up in the middle of the city. The food options were enough and service was quick and friendly. This place is ideal for girl friends or a date.

I love a cool garden setting and my friend decided to bring me here! What a lovely ambience filled with artificial flowers, lights & big windows to look out to the greenery! Truly lovely aside from the loud chatter of guests near me! Wished i could hear jazz or instrumental music to enhance the ambience. Bass was too heavy. The food was good especially the crab cake and grilled salmon! The plus point of this place is the surrounding! A change of music to something more “European” would truly be more enriching! I would come back and if it’s cool and breezy, i’d sit outdoors away from the higher decibels 🤭
Washroom facilities are cool and clean! Artsy stairwell!

A wonderful cozy space in a very convenient location. Even before you get here, you can smell the food from outside. I had the rose latte and it did not disappoint. I loved it! The place is gorgeous - a definite photo op! The service is good and food arrives quickly. I was lucky to get a seat without reservation but I would recommend booking in advance because every other table had a reserved sign. I had the crisp mid wings and those were nice as well!

The interior of the cafe has an ethereal floral fairy vibe. I love how you feel absolutely immersed in a floral fantasy. Even the tables were a glass table top encasement display of flowers. Their crab cake is a must-try. They were generous with their ingredients. When you bite in, you can taste the shreds of crab meat. The beautiful food presentation makes the enjoyment of each bite even more delightful. I also enjoyed their Lemon Cloud Matcha. The tartness of the thick lemon cloud added an interesting twist to the matcha. A great way to end your meal with this refreshing drink.

Tiong Bahru Bakery - Fort Caninning Park

Rain or sun, you will enjoy your stay here, at the foothills of a beautiful park, surrounded by trees.

FRESHLY DELICIOUS:
The concept and atmosphere unique. They offer great selections of new and classic bakery items. As well as, great hot and iced beverage options.

CLASSICALLY NEW:
I ordered new and classic delicious bakery items.

SALMON:
If you appreciate a good and fresh salmon, may I recommend you try, Smocked Salmon & Cream Cheese Croissant - S$12.

I love a good natural salmon and they offered a great choice here. It tasted fresh and the combination of ingredients was delicious and unique. Be sure to ask them to toast it for you, as they offered it for me.

CHOCOLAT DEVOTION:
Next, if you love a good Chocolat pastry, may I suggest you try, Pain Au Chocolat - S$4.70. Just as delicious as it looks.

SMOOTH MATCHA:
Finally, if you’re a lover of all things Matcha, you should try their, ALL-NEW: Matcha Basque Cheesecake - S$9. This one I’d say was exquisite and very unique.

The texture, the flavor was nothing short of delicious. With each bite it was smooth, soft and rich. Try it out and your taste buds will be on cloud nine.

SEATING:
Good seating options too, both indoor and a large patio. I stayed here for few hours and enjoyed hearing great music through their BOSE speakers. I also enjoyed listening to loud thunder and rain outside, beating against the treetops.

OVERALL:
It’s a laid back and relaxing experience with super friendly and welcoming staff. If you need any assistance to place your order, they’re here to assist with a smile. I’m looking forward returning.

This branch of Tiong Bahru bakery is spacious and had a lot of available seating. They had indoor and open air seats. Of course, the croissants and drinks are great! I ordered pistachio matcha & strawberry lychee rose croissant, both of which I haven't seen at TBB before. Both were really good!

Not your typical shopping mall TBB since it was nestled at the Foothills of Fort Canning. Chill vibes amidst lots of nature and it wasn't too crowded even on a Saturday afternoon :) The latte art was beautiful and the mocha and hot chocolate tasted really good. Love their pastries, beautiful plates and the strong air conditioning too. They also have a water dispenser so you can really spend an entire afternoon here after you are done with your drink! Service charge and GST are chargeable so it can be pretty pricey - however it's a nice treat for yourself once in a while!

I was strolling around the area of Fort Canning Park when I caught the aroma of freshly baked pastries that seemed to have just come out of the oven. The smell immediately attracted me, so I decided to step into this cafe. Even though I arrived in the morning, there were already several customers inside waiting in line. I joined the queue as well, but fortunately I did not have to wait long before being served by a very friendly and kind waitress.

When I was about to order, she realized that I do not eat pork or consume alcohol (as I wear a hijab), so she immediately recommended a croissant filled with roast beef and tomatoes. I also asked for extra cheese. It tasted absolutely delicious.
I also ordered bottled juice because the weather in Singapore was extremely hot that day, and I was already feeling a bit tired after walking around the Fort Canning area. The juice turned out to be very refreshing, although quite sour. The café also provides complimentary tap water, which made the experience even more comfortable.

I sat by the window with a lovely view of the park, and it was such a pleasant experience. This café has become one of my favorite cafés in the Fort Canning area.

Tiong Bahru Bakery is a great stop for fresh pastries and a relaxed breakfast.
The croissants are excellent here: buttery, flaky and baked perfectly. I especially loved the seasonal flavours like the lychee-strawberry rose croissant, which looks beautiful and tastes just as good. The almond croissant is also a must-try, very crisp on the outside and soft inside.

The coffee is smooth and well made, and the outdoor seating area has a really calm, green atmosphere. It is a nice place to sit for a while, enjoy your pastry and take a break. Service is quick even when it gets busy, and everything feels freshly prepared.

If you love pastries, this is one of the most reliable and enjoyable bakeries in Singapore.

First time at L’Amo Bistrò del Mare and it was a nice experience overall.

The Boston lobster pasta was a highlight, very fresh and sweet lobster paired with pasta cooked in well balanced sauce that was rich yet not heavy. The wagyu was tender and buttery. The lamb was also excellent, well seasoned and nicely done without being gamey.

Service was attentive without being intrusive and friendly throughout. Although expected, they still managed to surprise us with a complimentary cake for our anniversary which was a thoughtful touch.

The tiramisu was prepared tableside, but the cocoa dusting was a bit too heavy compared to the usual light dusting. That said, it was still a nice end to the meal, especially with the complimentary cake.

Overall, great food, nice atmosphere, and good service. Would come back again.

**⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ An Unforgettable Lunch**

I recently enjoyed a fantastic lunch at L'Amo Bistro Del Mare and was thoroughly impressed.
We started with the Kingfish Crudo paired with marinated strawberries, it was incredibly refreshing and flavorful. For our mains, the **Cacio e Pepe** and the **Tagliolini al Limone with Caviar** were cooked to perfection.

The service was a highlight, too. Mark and Alvin were fantastic—attentive and friendly throughout the meal.

You can't miss the Tiramisu—intense coffee flavor, fluffy cream, and generous chocolate layers. They even assemble it right at your table, adding a wonderful touch of theater to the experience.

Highly recommended for a top-tier Italian lunch. I can’t wait to return!

Beautiful venue, great ambiance, amazing food & exceptional service! My friend and I were sharing two mains and Hari even offered to split the dishes for us, which we appreciated a lot. Would def come back again.

Had an outstanding experience at L’Amo Bistro del Mare! The restaurant itself was elegant & cozy plus they even had a grand piano & a pianist playing live music for the evening.

Food wise everything was pretty good,  oysters were fresh and the Bluefin tuna carpaccio was super refreshing.

Our highlight was definitely the boston lobster pasta which really hit all the spots, from the taste to presentation. Fun fact - They have a nice seafood display from which you may choose the various shellfish or fishes to be cooked accordingly, all of which were reasonably priced too. Will definitely be back to try some of their fishes next time.

Lastly I highly recommend saving space for their tiramisu, which was to my surprise assembled tableside, a fun gesture for us to see how the dessert comes to life.

Service team was top notch - especially from Ramoni whom was friendly, professional, knowledgeable & on point with all her recommendations.

Overall a great experience. Highly recommended to try and i cannot wait to return.

My husband & I dined at L’Amo last night for our anniversary and it completely blew us away. The level of service was on another level, from the first hello at the door to the service team, the sommelier, and the floor manager, every interaction felt thoughtful, warm, and genuinely sincere. The food was insanely good, infact one of the best restaurants I’ve dined in.  Everything was beautifully explained and packed with flavour. The gamberoni, polpo al josper & the tagliatelle alla puttanesca deserves a special mention, absolutely outstanding!! Do yourself a favour and order it if you ever give this place a visit.

What really stood out though was the team. The people at Lamo are insanely driven and passionate about what they do, and it shows. Massive shoutout to Navin, Viknes, Hari, Ramani, Kalai, and Mark, you guys were incredible and truly made the experience what it was. Service like this is rare, and it made the entire experience unforgettable.

Paragon has been a familiar hangout spot for me for many years, and it’s still one of Orchard Road’s most dependable malls. It has a classy, spacious feel and remains a great place to spend time whether you’re shopping, meeting friends, or just taking a slow walk indoors

There’s a strong mix of retail stores, good eateries, and practical conveniences all under one roof. I also like that it has Cold Storage Gold / Fresh for quality groceries, which makes it more than just a shopping mall. The presence of medical clinics adds to the convenience too

This is a modern upscale 6 level shopping mall right in the heart of Orchard Road. Most people who shop here knows what they want and they just go straight to the shop. Others come here for their lunch or simply have tea here for nice chit chats. Now’s the time for Chinese New Year shopping so the mall is beautifully decorated this year !! I love the beautiful lights !!! And can feel the Chinese New Year vibes and mood !!! I came here to buy my CNY clothes at Metro Paragon on level two. It’s been a wonderful and fruitful trip !! Bought 6 pieces 👍👍👍💗💗💗

A wonderful and good shopping experience having good brands like Prada and Gucci in them. It also has amazing food and restaurant options on the basement level. The best food you could find is the fish and Coast company which serves authentic fish to perfection and also the area has some good Italian food joints, and some nice Asian and local cuisine as well. It also has a nice supermarket to buy daily essentials and fresh fruits.

Stepping into Paragon feels like entering a high-end gallery. The layout is spacious and intuitive, featuring polished marble floors and a glass-fronted facade that has become a landmark on Orchard Road.

​Key Highlights
-​Luxury Fashion: The mall is synonymous with high fashion, housing iconic multi-story flagships for Gucci, Prada, and Givenchy, alongside luxury heavyweights like Saint Laurent, Balenciaga, and Loewe.

-​The "Junior" Floor: Level 5 is a dedicated sanctuary for families, featuring high-end children’s labels like Armani Junior and Petit Bateau, complemented by a free indoor playground and top-tier enrichment centers.

-​Exquisite Dining: From the Michelin-starred Crystal Jade Golden Palace to the legendary Imperial Treasure Super Peking Duck, the culinary offerings lean heavily toward quality. For something more casual yet chic, PS.Cafe and the basement's Din Tai Fung are perennial favorites.

-​Medical Wellness: Uniquely, the mall is connected to Paragon Medical, a premium tower housing world-class specialists and aesthetic clinics, allowing visitors to combine wellness appointments with luxury retail.

Paragon Shopping Centre is one of my favourite malls along Orchard Road when I feel like doing some upscale shopping. I enjoy browsing the luxury brands here—it’s elegant but still comfortable to walk around. The basement has plenty of nice food choices too, whether it’s grabbing a quick bite or sitting down for a proper meal. I also like that the mall has a supermarket and even a medical wing with specialist clinics, so it’s really convenient for almost everything.

Clarke Quay is one of the most energetic and colorful areas in Singapore, especially in the evening. The riverside setting, combined with the bright neon lights and lively atmosphere, makes it a fantastic place to walk around and enjoy the city at night.
There are plenty of restaurants, bars, and entertainment options, ranging from casual dining to upscale spots with beautiful waterfront views. The colorful canopy overhead adds a unique architectural touch and helps keep the area comfortable even in warmer weather.
Whether you're looking for a relaxed dinner by the river, live music, or just a scenic stroll along the Singapore River, Clarke Quay delivers. It’s clean, well organized, and feels safe — perfect for couples, friends, or even families earlier in the evening.
Highly recommended if you want to experience Singapore’s nightlife in a vibrant and picturesque setting.

I visited Clarke Quay during the day and found it to be a really charming place. It offers a wide variety of restaurants, making it a great spot to explore different dining options all in one area. The colorful buildings and riverside setting give it a very cute and vibrant atmosphere.

Although I went during the daytime, I can imagine it becomes even more lively and visually appealing at night, when all the venues are open and the lights reflect beautifully along the river. Definitely a place worth visiting, especially in the evening for the full experience.

Clarke Quay is a vibrant riverside destination that’s very popular with tourists, offering a huge selection of bars and restaurants for nightlife and dining. The atmosphere really picks up in the evening with the music and light. It is a great spot whether you're looking for light bites or a full night out.

As a local, I appreciate that it’s well maintained and clean. It is a good place for an evening stroll or leisurely walk. While it feels touristy and the prices are definitely on the higher side, it’s still a solid spot to soak in the city’s energy and enjoy a wide variety of food and drink in one location.

Clarke Quay is one of those places in Singapore that truly comes alive after sunset. Set along the river, the area buzzes with neon lights, upbeat music, and a constant flow of people soaking in the atmosphere. Lined with a wide mix of bars, pubs, clubs, and restaurants, it offers something for everyone—whether you’re in the mood for a relaxed drink by the water or a high-energy night out with loud beats and dancing crowds.

The riverside setting adds a charming touch, making evening walks especially enjoyable. You can stroll along the promenade, watch boats glide past, and take in the colorful reflections dancing on the water. There’s always something happening—live music spilling out of venues, street performers adding flair, and groups of friends laughing their way from one spot to another.

Yes, it does feel a little touristy, with busy crowds and popular chains, but that’s part of its appeal. The energy is infectious, the vibe is festive, and the options are endless. If you’re looking for nightlife, people-watching, and a fun place to unwind after dark, Clarke Quay delivers the experience with style and spirit
Nice area to wander under canopy, shelter from the sun, socialise, have light / western food. A great way of spending a few hours in the afternoon. Especially from the bars at the river edge.

As a tourist, found this such a great place to buy interesting snacks and food to try and also to bring home for others. It was a bit tricky to find in Chinatown but we had the help of having someone who had visited before. Easy enough to get to from the train. Quite affordable and definitely cheaper than buying lots of souvenirs. I loved that they had a dedicated section for tourists too with tea, chocolate, biscuits etc. We spent ages here looking at all the different products, it's huge!

February 2026 visit:
Shop in basement level. I was asked to get several spice packs of bkt (Bak Kut Teh) to bring home.

This shop has plenty of brands and options in 10pk or gift packs and multi pack sets. Located at the entrance and down the soup aisle.

Plenty of other goods, but I was simply there for the BKT spices.
It’s right in front of the accommodation, so it‘s convenient to go shopping and there are many places to eat, so I used it often throughout the trip.

If u don't have Singapore dollar, no worry, the boss is good and they willing to take the Malaysia ringgit after currency conversion, add extra for them for money changer exchange will do. God bless the senior boss for their kind heart.

I’ve visited this FairPrice quite a few times throughout the last 5+ years and I’ve always been around the area and needed a water bottle or a little snack but everytime it’s been a pleasant experience. They’ve also got self-service cashiers.

The FairPrice is located on the lowest floor of Chinatown Point next to a Subway and a food court. The FairPrice is a decent size with it’s meat, fish, vegetable, fruit, candy, snacks, alcohol, beverages, other normal food items sections. Sometimes there are good discounts for certain items as well. Aisles are clean and organized, staff are friendly and help you when you’re looking for something, etc.

Very well stocked Fairprice supermarket at the heart of Bukit Timah! Went there for grocery shopping and they have everything I need with great prices! Ongoing offers are addictive and makes you want to be there everyday to find the best deals like how locals like it. Be sure to sign up for their membership as there may be ongoing rewards and save the hassle of paying by using their mobile app!

My wife and I were here today (11 DECEMBER 2025) to do some grocery shopping. We are both regular customers here and ever since it opened here at Bukit Timah Plaza. It is a HUGE NTUC supermarket - or I should say "FairPrice Finest - with a super wide variety of products and well stocked over two levels connected within by a pair of ascending and descending escalators. Service here is often very good - with its friendly and helpful staff. And the shopping ambience here is also very good with wide enough aisles and in air-conditioned comfort.

FairPrice Finest has a surprisingly beautiful flower selection. The blooms are fresh, well-arranged, and thoughtfully curated from elegant bouquets to simple everyday flowers. You can tell care is taken in both quality and presentation. It's always my go-to when I want something classy without overpaying.

I enjoy shopping at FairPrice Finest Bukit Timah Plaza.The store is very spacious and well-stocked with a wide variety of the product.It is 24 hours ,which is extremely convenient for those who need to shop late at night . Night time entrance is located at B2,and the store is easy to navigate. Night time shopping is especially pleasant because it is usually not very busy, allowing for a more relaxed experience.
A convenient and comfortable supermarket ,Highly recommended.

We have been shopping for a number of years and always find the place the most pleasant Fair Price in Singapore.
Today we were served by Ms Tan Choo Sun at the checkout.  When we were packing our buys, she helped us repacked  so that the fragile items are safe. When I was asking myself where to put the bag of prawns which are a bit wet, she quickly went over to the next cashier to get a plastic bag to put the wet items.
Kudos to her and Ntuc BTP!

I visited Cold Storage on Thursday to purchase a large potted plant, and I was truly impressed by the excellent customer service.

The plant didn't have a price tag, so the manager and Leyi immediately offered to help check the price for me. Instead of asking me to wait around, they told me I could continue with my shopping. Once they received the pricing information, they kindly contacted me and asked me to return to complete the purchase.

What impressed me even more was that they helped place the heavy plant onto their trolley, making it so much easier for me to wheel it to my car. Their kindness, patience, and willingness to go the extra mile made the whole shopping experience smooth and enjoyable.

A big thank you to the manager and especially Leyi for the outstanding service. It's staff like them who make customers want to come back again. Keep up the great work!

Nice, big and spacious. Great place to shop for groceries.
For vegans and vegetarians, there are also plenty of plant-based stuff.

Huge variety of well displayed items. But due to the manpower shortages, some expired products and rotten fruits are not well spotted.

Like their weekly discount shelves and also the big snack counter.

Cashier system has self checked which is v much convenient.

Helpful staff.

Surprised to see this high-end supermarket all the way in West Coast. Perhaps most of the consumers are folks who live in condo complexes nearby between West Coast and NUS campus.

The items tend to be expensive and sometimes rare items not commonly found in other supermarkets, even the likes of FairPrice Finest. The deli section has a lot of expensive meat grills and imported hams, some as far as places like Italy (some PDO stuffs). The beer section also contains craft beers I've almost never seen elsewhere in Singapore, like Japanese craft beers and Trappists.

The sushi section is also pretty well-stocked, fresh, and relatively affordable. The inari sushi trio is particularly cheap and makes a nice lunch (my favorite).

A nice clean supermarket that’s bright, airy and not overly crowded. A fair amount of their products are imported, so it does leave an impression that it is slightly more expensive compared to the other Supermarket chains.

However, based on my purchases, the differences were negligible, so your experience may vary. I like that despite it not being massive, they have a cold cuts deli, a chicken rotisserie, ribs, sushi counter, even live lobsters and oysters all within reach of each other.

Staff are also willingly to point out where particular items might be. Not a bad place to shop.
Nice place to shopping, can cook instant noodles with fresh egg, wow !!
Overpriced. Even more expensive than 7eleven. Seriously?

This mart was really a Savior for us.We could keep our luggage here via the bounce app at a reasonable cost.

This was very near to our stay and being in the main place is a plus point.

The items here are much cheaper than other supermarkets.We bought ourself a bread , iced tea at very reasonable cost.

Staff friendly.

Quick ready to eat food items with water heater and microwave facilities are available.

Staffed at 10am already on Sunday! Kid needed Mayonnaise for leftover potatoes wedges because too spicy and even 7-11 didn't carry. I walked a little further and found it! A familiar brand no less: expiration end of the year so can last quite a while!!! Staff very friendly and offered credit card payment when I realized NETS card I had wouldn't work and trying not to use cash. 👏🫡🙏

This item is a favourite of my kids and none of the major supermarkets like NTUC had any available stocks either online or the physical stores. I went to a few of the usual supermarkets that I buy them from but alas they didn’t have them. A quick google search and U Mart had it online and in store. Made my way down to the Boat Quay branch and they had more than enough stocks of it. Their drinks and other items are priced so competitively as well. This is the mart U need to head to for your snacks and drinks fix.
Very clean, very well organized, very... empty. Yes! 1/2 of the store is empty. So you have also in high peak time a lot of space to buy your thinks
Very interesting place for sure! Though it was rather empty inside, Prices are rather affordable especially for this area of town they have drinks snacks and alcohol and some bite size stuff

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Tried some of their food during boutique fair 2025. It was ok, but it’s really nothing special considering the price. The har gao and siew Mai are quite mushy. Kong Bak Pao isn’t fantastic either, just normal. Noodles and dumpling are alright.

Tried their food again at their original location and still not impressed with it especially the Har Gao. Skin is too thick and meat is not substantial. The rest are also mediocre.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Bought the festive premium pork feasting platter for christmas and the only thing that is premium is the word premium printed on the label. I followed the instructions on the packaging for preparation. Instructions were easy to follow, but that was the only good thing in this meal.

The ribs were extremely tough - worse than the ones you get at cold storage. Under seasoned and had to add my own bbq sauce to make it somewhat edible. Ate 2 ribs and threw the rest away.

The porchetta is fatty and bland. Ate one mouthful and threw the rest away.

Sausages were too salty.

Meatballs were ok, but you can probably get the same quality from the super market.

For $82, you’re better off spending your money elsewhere. I dare say Mcdonald’s and Saizeriya tastes better than this.

This might be the worst Christmas meal I’ve ever had in my life. Perhaps maybe even one of the worst meals I’ve ever had in my life.

The company should stop selling these sets immediately.

Would have given -100 stars on google reviews if google allowed me to, but I guess I’ll have to settle for 1 star which in my opinion, is too generous.

Thanks for ruining my Christmas. You guys should rename your company to “The Grinch”.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: Ah Ma’s handmade Yong Tau Foo it’s really bad. I couldn’t even go for second bite. The fish paste inside was really hard as well.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
First time online ordering from them. Usually I went to the physical store.

Have to compliment them on how well the items were packed. The green were in a recycle bag And there’s ice pack in between each layers of frozen items.

My daughter loves their Siew Mai and I love the garlic chilli. 🥰 Oh! The pork ribs are really fresh too. Used it to cook bbq ribs and it’s really yummy.
Trying some new items this round. Looking forward to more purchase with them.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: The Signature Pineapple Tart tin is not sealed. Inside it is a ziplock bag (also with no safety seal) containing the tarts. Quality of the tarts is below expectation too, since the small tin is priced at almost $28. Feels like this shop had bought some cheap tarts and repacked it for resale.

Just have our dinner here at Shampoo Food Centre. Here you have a wide varieties of food to suit everybody...just like any food court. We have fried hokkien noodles from this Singapore Fried Hokkien Noodles. Here you must prepare to queue for it cos it is always long queue. However, guarantee you will love it hence dunt mind to wait in the queue for it. Whenever we come here is alway must eat this fried hokkien noodles without fail. ( See attached Photo) In addition, it is also a must have this egg oyster dish from this Just Heng Fried Oyster stall. Previously, was run by a old uncle who passed down his trade to his daughter in law who has learnt the trade from him. She is equally good as her father in law who of course is the best. (Photo attached) There is also this Nadi Lemak stall here who sell a good NasiLemak with wide range of dishes to go with it besides it's normal ikan billis with a good taste of it's chilli. Of course you can end your dinner with some cold desserts such as ice kachang, red beans soup, green beans soup, and of course there is also a Chendol store here that sell solely Chendol. You guys must come here to try the good, scrumtious and mouth watering food here. You sure will enjoy it....

This is a stall selling Fish Soup Noddle. Each time I visit, there is always more than 10 pax in the queue. Kindly note that the stall opens 0900-1400hrs or sold out which ever comes first. Also, it is closed on Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

Today, I queued for 30 minutes, sat down to enjoy the delicious soup noodles and the stall shouted out Sold Out at 1230hrs.

This place gave a retro atmosphere. Sitting down for a meal bring black memories of childhood days.

The food centre is not huge yet it compasses a large variety of food selection.

Hokkein fried mee, fish slice soup / porridge, mix pork organs soup, chicken rice, duck rice,laksa, lor mee, sugar cane drink, chendol and etc.

Another over-rated food centre. Contrary to many reviews posted here, I have yet to have something decent at this food centre. The only reason I come here is because the wanton mee that I use to eat while growing up has relocated here. And this wanton mee is not spectacular. It is an acquired taste. The chicken rice here is underwhelming. I have tried 2 stalls here and I can say that I will never ever eat chicken rice here again. Parking here is also a pain. Lots in front of the food centre is never available. It might be best to park at the back around the ntuc area and make your own way here.

This market is a treasure trove of authentic local fare, brimming with vibrant, down-to-earth charm. Two standout gems are Xin Ji Congee and its adjacent soup stall, which have become my go-to for hearty, flavorful meals. I’ve visited the market twice specifically to savor Xin Ji’s congee, which boasts an exceptional wok hei that elevates its taste to new heights. The pork stomach soup is a steal at S$5, generously portioned with fresh ingredients, and comes with the added perk of free soup refills, courtesy of the friendly owner. Completing the culinary experience is the nearby chendol shop—with a concise 4-item menu that delivers nothing but perfectly executed, satisfying treats.
Pull and push up bars situated in the middle of the park. Some shade nearby to escape the blazing sun. Perfect for a calisthenics workout. The park is a great place to run as well.
They removed dip rails and added some silly kids toys 🤷‍♂️🤦‍♂️now the only useful stuff for calisthenics is a pull-up bar

Nice exercise station / outdoor gym in Fort Canning Park. It's atop a flight of steps so after climbing up the flight you can push yourself further by doing pull-ups, sit-ups & other static or other exercises here. Exercise here with an amazing view 😍 open 24 hours, free of charge. Thank you NParks!
Closed for ugrading. Only opens 1st Q 2025
So strong! Yea
The platform with two slides side by side

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

I attended a deepavalli celebration organized by Sinda. Buffet dinner, goodie bag and a gift card to purchase items from ntuc. There were songs and dance entertainments. Well organized. Thank you Sinda for bringing cheers to low income residents during this festival of light. Well done. Keep up the good work 🙏

Telok Blangah Community Club is a community centre serving Telok Blangah residents, it has several fitness, sporting and community gathering facilities.

There is an Old Chang Kee bistro on the ground floor.

Singapore have really great community clubs, this particular one is small scale as this is an old housing area. Still, this club house is newly and well renovated, with well planned facilities and rooms. Love it that old chang kee is here for breakfast or snack.
Nostalgic for me, I grew up in this area.
Came here for a toastmasters event. Very nice community Center. Great toastmasters club
Has a basketball court and 2 indoor badminton courts. Spacious and also has old Chang Kee and vending machines.

I had such a fantastic time learning Wing Chun here! The instructor Master 师父 Ken was incredibly skilled and patient, making the whole experience enjoyable and educational. I'm already feeling more confident in my movements. Highly recommend! 🥋✨👍
Volunteered here at the give & take market and everyone is so welcoming. You can tell that the staff just want to do a great job to serve the residents and they have such big compassionate hearts!
Held our daughter wedding ceremony at the multi purpose hall. The staff in attendance were welcoming and friendly. Appreciated with thanks towards the management and staff.
Staff named Bee Hoon is extremely rude, sound impatient and unhelpful. Community Centre are meant to help and advice the community staying in the area. Pls understand that!
The hawker center is so vested and plenty of different cultural foods.

This park is a lot of fun for kids of all ages. They have swings, wooden log obstacle courses, and big slides. It can get crowded, but it's worth it. Even older kids will find things to enjoy. The play areas are designed to be fun and engaging, with different activities to suit different interests. Kids can easily move from one activity to the next, and parents can relax and supervise in the green spaces and scenic spots. The park is a good choice for a family day out, offering something for everyone. You can create some nice memories with your kids here, and the family can have a good time together. The atmosphere is relaxed, and the scenery is nice. Overall, it's a great place to spend the day with the family. The kids will have fun, and you'll appreciate the park's design and amenities. It's a spot that's sure to leave you feeling satisfied after a day out with the family. With its variety of activities and relaxed atmosphere, this park is a great option for families.

🌿Jubilee Park is located at the western slope of Fort Canning Park.
🛝The place has been repurposed into an exciting sand-filled Playground for children with Slides that is built into the slopes of the hill terrain, Swings, Seesaw, log climb, etc, that children can have plenty of great fun outdoors.
Fun note:
🏆Jubilee Park used to be known as King George the Fifth Jubilee Park or King George V Park.
🎎It is name as such to mark the silver jubilee of King George the Fifth  and Queen Mary.
🏆There were a National Theatre, a River Valley Swimming Pool and a Van Cliff Aquarium.
These recreational facilities had all been demolished.

Really nice open space to relax and just take a break. The playground area is well designed and fun, and the views around the park are calm and peaceful. Good spot to just walk around or hang out for a bit.

2/20/2024 I and my husband come in and walk around this area, I think the location of this section is very convenient, close to the subway station MRT, there are many landscape and a variety of plants and flowers. It's a great place to walk, take photos, play with kids, Very good choice! I like the environment and enjoy it. But if visit on the night, you should bring some friends, not only you.

First time checking out this park. Beautiful spacious surroundings with a small public car park next to it.
A fun wooden obstacle course greets us as we walk toward thr park from the car park. Next to it there are a couple of inclusive swing (for the kids who are wheelchair bound) Nice!! And there are about another 5 swings just round the corner.
Further up, there are 5 different slides for the kids to play on. Spotted two sets of see-saw under a partially shaded area.
In case you run out of water, we spotted two re-filling stations around the park. There is even taps for the little ones to wash their hands and feet after playing on the sand.
And if u are hungry after all the chasing and playing, Tiong Bahru Bakery is just nearby. Follow the aromatic smell of bread & coffee. 😉
Note: Do come earlier in the morning as it can gets hot by 1030am.

The Fort Canning Tree Tunnel is definitely one of Singapore’s most iconic photo spots. The view looking up through the spiral staircase towards the lush greenery is beautiful and well worth seeing in person. It’s a unique spot that creates stunning photos, especially on a sunny day.

The only downside is the long queue, as many visitors are waiting for their turn to take photos. Be prepared to wait, especially during peak hours. That said, most people are patient and respectful, and the final result is worth it if you enjoy photography and sightseeing.

A must-visit spot in Singapore, just come early if you want to avoid the crowds!

Location and views are nice!
Bad experience with tourists however a lot of them are very rude and speak no English. Clearly signs that say no climbing over the edge to sit however they do this and line up for it so it causes lots of congestion especially at the bottom and on the actual stairs. Would recommend going very early to avoid crowds.

Lovely park to venture near City Hall. Visited the spice garden as well. The tunnel is very crowded with queues at ard 8am. Lots of local & foreign tourists there at the tunnel. Else where, there’s peace & quiet.

It's ok if you want to drop by.. But if purposely to come here far away to enjoy the view, it's not really worth it. Long queue during weekend. You still have to wait in queue for individual photography during weekdays. The park is not too small.

CROWDED - Probably the most we’ve waited in Singapore. It’s a beautiful spot but extremely difficult to take photos due to the crowds. We arrived here just gone 9am on a Saturday. Groups are sadly inconsiderate in that each person spends a number of minutes taking multiple photos stopping each time someone passes by the stairs. If you really wanted an image, earlier the better. Easy to find with public transport. We did not wait to take images on the steps as I can easily imagine this queue lasting into 45 mins+ and there are other things to see also
